steps: Prep the asparagus: Hold each asparagus spear near the bottom end and bend it until it naturally snaps, discarding the woody base. Slice the remaining tender stalks on a slight diagonal into uniform 2-inch pieces so they cook evenly.Heat the pan: Heat the olive oil and melt the butter together in a large skillet over medium-high heat until the mixture is hot and foaming.Sauté the vegetables: Add the 2-inch asparagus pieces to the skillet.

Sauté for 3 to 4 minutes, tossing frequently, until the asparagus turns a vibrant bright green color and begins to soften slightly.Add garlic and finish: Stir in the garlic, salt, and black pepper. Cook for an additional 1 to 2 minutes, tossing constantly, until the garlic is fragrant and the asparagus is crisp-tender.
Remove from heat, immediately drizzle with fresh lemon juice, top with Parmesan cheese if using, and serve hot.

title: ROASTED BUTTERNUT SQUASH

ingredients.name: maple syrup or honey (optional, for extra caramelization)
fresh thyme leaves (or 1/2 tsp dried thyme)

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Cut off the top and bottom of the butternut squash, peel off the skin with a vegetable peeler, slice it in half lengthwise, and scoop out the seeds.
Cut the flesh into uniform 1-inch cubes.Toss with seasoning: Place the squash cubes directly onto a large rimmed baking sheet. Drizzle with the olive oil and maple syrup if using.

Sprinkle evenly with the fresh thyme, salt, black pepper, and optional cinnamon or nutmeg. Toss thoroughly until well coated.Spread and roast: Arrange the squash cubes in a single flat layer, leaving space between the pieces so they roast instead of steaming.

Roast in the preheated oven for 25 to 30 minutes, tossing them halfway through, until the squash is fork-tender and the edges are beautifully browned and caramelized.Garnish and serve: Remove from the oven and transfer the hot roasted squash to a serving platter. Adjust seasoning with an extra pinch of salt if desired, and serve immediately.

title: PEACH COBBLER
steps: Gather all ingredients.
Preheat the oven to 425 degrees F (220 degrees C).

Combine peaches, 1/4 cup white sugar, 1/4 cup brown sugar, 1/4 teaspoon cinnamon, nutmeg, lemon juice, and cornstarch in a large bowl; toss to coat evenly, and pour into a 2-quart baking dish. Bake in preheated oven for 10 minutes.

Meanwhile, combine flour, 1/4 cup white sugar, 1/4 cup brown sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl. Blend in butter with your fingertips or a pastry blender until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Stir in water until just combined.
Remove peaches from oven, and drop spoonfuls of topping over them.
Mix 3 tablespoons white sugar and 1 teaspoon cinnamon together in a small bowl; sprinkle over entire cobbler.
Bake in the preheated oven until topping is golden, about 30 minutes.

title: GERMAN POTATO SALAD

ingredients.name: (approx. 1 kg) small red potatoes or Yukon Gold potatoes
Dijon mustard or whole grain mustard

steps: Cook the potatoes: Place scrubbed whole potatoes into a large pot and cover with cold water by an inch. Add a tablespoon of kosher salt.

Bring to a boil over medium-high heat and cook for 15 to 20 minutes until fork-tender. Drain, let cool slightly, and slice into 1/4-inch thick rounds.Crisp the bacon: Place diced bacon into a large, unheated skillet over medium heat.
Fry for 8 to 10 minutes until completely crispy. Transfer bacon to a paper towel-lined plate with a slotted spoon.

Leave 3 to 4 tablespoons of rendered bacon fat in the skillet, discarding any excess.Build the tangy dressing: Lower skillet heat to medium and add diced onion to the hot bacon grease. Sauté for 3 to 4 minutes until translucent, then stir in minced garlic for 30 seconds.

Pour in apple cider vinegar, water or broth, sugar, mustard, salt, and pepper. Simmer and scrape up browned bits until the sugar dissolves.Toss and serve: Add warm sliced potatoes directly into the skillet with the dressing.
Toss gently for 1 minute so the potatoes absorb the warm vinaigrette. Remove from heat, fold in most of the crispy bacon and fresh herbs, then garnish with the remaining bacon and serve warm.

title: CHILLED MELON SOUP WITH PROSCIUTTO CHIP

ingredients.name: large cantaloupe (approx. 3-4 lbs, peeled, seeded, and chopped)
honey (optional, adjust based on melon sweetness)
heavy cream or full-fat crème fraîche

steps: Crisp the prosciutto: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper and lay the prosciutto slices flat.

Bake for 6 to 8 minutes until crisp and darkened. Transfer to a paper towel to cool, then break into large "chips".Blend the soup: Place the chopped cantaloupe, fresh lime juice, honey, and mint leaves into a high-speed blender.

Blend on high for 1 to 2 minutes until completely smooth, frothy, and uniform in color.Chill thoroughly: Pour the melon purée into a large glass pitcher or airtight container. Cover and refrigerate for at least 2 hours, or until thoroughly chilled.Assemble and serve: Stir the chilled soup well and ladle it evenly into four chilled bowls.

Drizzle each bowl with a swirl of heavy cream and a few drops of olive oil. Top with a pinch of flaky sea salt, cracked black pepper, a fresh mint leaf, and stand a crispy prosciutto chip upright in the center.

title: BAKED CHICKEN IN CREAMY BASIL AND CORN SAUCE

ingredients.name: skinless chicken breasts (pounded to even thickness)
fresh corn kernels (or thawed frozen corn)
fresh basil leaves (thinly sliced or chiffonade)

steps: Season and sear chicken: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Rub the chicken breasts with olive oil, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat a large, oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Sear the chicken for 2 to 3 minutes on each side until golden brown.

Remove chicken and set aside on a plate (it will finish cooking in the oven).Sauté aromatics and corn: Reduce the skillet heat to medium. Melt the butter, then add the chopped shallot and minced garlic.

Sauté for 1 minute until fragrant. Add the corn kernels to the pan and cook for 3 to 4 minutes until they begin to soften and lightly brown.Build the creamy sauce: Pour the heavy cream and chicken broth into the skillet, stirring to scrape up any browned bits from the bottom.

Bring to a simmer, then stir in the grated Parmesan cheese until completely melted and smooth. Simmer for 2 minutes to let the sauce thicken slightly.Bake and garnish: Nestled the seared chicken breasts back into the corn sauce, spooning some sauce over the top.
Transfer the skillet directly into the preheated oven. Bake for 12 to 15 minutes until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C).
Remove from the oven, stir in the fresh lemon juice, top generously with the fresh basil, and serve hot.

title: SAUTEED BOK CHOY

ingredients.name: halved or quartered lengthwise and washed thoroughly

steps: Prep and dry: Trim the very bottom ends of the baby bok choy heads. Cut them in half or quarters lengthwise.

Rinse them thoroughly under cold running water to remove any trapped dirt between the leaves, then shake or pat completely dry.Sauté the aromatics: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the sliced garlic and minced ginger.

Cook for 30 to 45 seconds, stirring constantly, until fragrant but before the garlic begins to brown.Sear the bok choy: Place the bok choy pieces into the skillet in a single layer, cut-side down if possible. Let them sear undisturbed for 1 to 2 minutes until the edges begin to turn golden brown.Steam and finish: Pour in the soy sauce and water, then immediately cover the skillet with a lid to trap the steam.

Let cook covered for 2 minutes until the green leaves are wilted and the white stalks are crisp-tender. Remove the lid, stir in the toasted sesame oil, transfer to a serving platter, and garnish with sesame seeds.

title: ROASTED CARROTS

ingredients.name: peeled and sliced diagonally into 1-inch thick pieces
fresh thyme leaves (or 1/2 tsp dried thyme)

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Peel the carrots, trim off the ends, and slice them on a sharp diagonal into thick 1-inch chunks so they maintain a meaty texture when cooked.Toss with oil and spices: Place the carrot pieces directly onto a large rimmed baking sheet.

Drizzle with the olive oil and melted butter, then sprinkle evenly with the thyme, salt, and black pepper. Toss thoroughly until every piece is uniformly coated.Arrange and roast: Spread the carrots out into a single flat layer, leaving space between the pieces so they roast and caramelize instead of steaming.

Roast in the preheated oven for 20 to 25 minutes, tossing them halfway through, until they are fork-tender and wrinkled with deeply browned edges.Garnish and serve: Remove the baking sheet from the oven. Transfer the hot roasted carrots to a serving platter, garnish generously with the fresh chopped parsley, and serve

title: ORANGE CUSTARD WITH BLACKBERRY

ingredients.name: fresh lemon juiceFinish: Mint leaves for garnish

steps: Whisk egg mixture: Whisk the egg yolks, ⅓ cup sugar, cornstarch, and salt together in a medium bowl until the mixture becomes pale yellow, thick, and completely smooth. Set aside.Infuse the dairy: Combine the whole milk, heavy cream, and orange zest in a medium saucepan.

Heat over medium heat until the liquid begins to steam and bubbles form around the edges (do not let it boil). Remove from heat.Temper and thicken: Slowly drizzle the hot milk mixture into the egg mixture, whisking constantly to prevent the eggs from scrambling.

Pour the combined mixture back into the saucepan. Cook over medium-low heat, whisking continuously for 3 to 5 minutes until the custard thickens into a pudding-like consistency.Chill and finish: Remove from heat and stir in the fresh orange juice until fully incorporated.

Pour the custard through a fine-mesh strainer into 4 individual ramekins or dessert cups. Press plastic wrap directly onto the surface of the custard to prevent a skin from forming, and refrigerate for at least 3 hours.Make compote and serve: While the custard chills, combine the blackberries, 1 tbsp sugar, and lemon juice in a small saucepan over medium-low heat.

Cook for 5 to 6 minutes, crushing some berries gently with a spoon, until a glossy syrup forms. Let cool completely, then spoon the blackberries over the chilled custards and garnish with mint before serving.

title: AVGOLEMONO SOUP (GREEK LEMON CHICKEN)

ingredients.name: uncooked long-grain white rice (or orzo pasta)
fresh squeezed lemon juice (approx. 2 large lemons)

steps: Cook the rice and chicken: Bring the chicken broth to a rolling boil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Stir in the rinsed white rice, reduce the heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the rice is tender.

Stir in the shredded cooked chicken and turn the heat to low to keep the soup hot but not boiling.Whisk the egg base: While the rice cooks, crack the eggs into a large mixing bowl. Whisk vigorously with a balloon whisk or hand mixer for 2 to 3 minutes until the eggs are completely broken down, frothy, and pale yellow.Add the lemon juice: Slowly pour the fresh lemon juice into the beaten eggs in a steady stream, whisking constantly so the acid does not curdle the eggs.Temper the sauce: Remove the soup pot from the heat source.
Ladle out about 2 cups of the hot chicken broth from the pot. While whisking the egg-lemon mixture continuously with one hand, slowly drizzle the hot broth into the bowl with the other hand.

This raises the temperature of the eggs gently so they do not scramble.Combine and thicken: Pour the tempered egg-lemon mixture back into the large pot with the remaining soup, stirring constantly. Place the pot back over low heat.
Cook, stirring continuously, for 3 to 5 minutes until the soup thickens into a rich, velvety consistency (do not let it boil, or the eggs will curdle). Stir in the salt, black pepper, and fresh dill.
Ladle into bowls and garnish with fresh lemon slices.

title: BEEF BRISKET BBQ

ingredients.name: whole packer beef brisket (12 to 14 lbs, trimmed with a 1/4-inch fat cap)
coarse freshly cracked black pepper (16-mesh preferred)
apple juice or apple cider vinegar (in a food-safe spray bottle)

steps: Trim and season: Trim the hard fat and silver skin from the brisket, leaving a uniform ¼-inch fat cap on the bottom. Mix the salt, black pepper, garlic powder, onion powder, and smoked paprika in a bowl.

Season the brisket generously on all sides, pressing the rub into the meat. Wrap in plastic wrap and refrigerate for 12 to 24 hours.Prep the smoker: Preheat your smoker to 225°F (107°C) using oak, hickory, or pecan wood.

Place the brisket inside the smoker, fat-side down (or fat-side up depending on your smoker's heat source directional flow), away from direct heat.Smoke and spritz: Smoke undisturbed for the first 3 hours to let the crust (bark) set. Afterward, spritz the meat with the apple juice every 1 hour to keep the surface moist.

Continue smoking for roughly 6 to 8 hours total, until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) and the bark is dark mahogany.Wrap and finish: Remove the brisket and wrap it tightly in butcher paper (or aluminum foil). Place it back into the smoker.

Continue cooking for another 3 to 4 hours until the internal temperature reaches 203°F (95°C) and a probe slides into the thickest part of the meat with no resistance like warm butter. Remove from the smoker, keep wrapped, and let rest in an empty insulated cooler for at least 1 to 2 hours before slicing against the grain.

title: GREEN AND YELLOW BEAN
steps: Blanch the beans: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Add the trimmed green and yellow beans to the pot and cook for 3 minutes until they turn vibrant in color and are crisp-tender.Ice bath shock: Immediately drain the beans and plunge them into a large bowl filled with ice water.

This stops the cooking process instantly and locks in their bright green and yellow colors. Leave for 2 minutes, then drain and pat completely dry.Sauté the aromatics: Heat the olive oil and melt the butter together in a large skillet over medium heat.

Add the minced shallot and sliced garlic, sautéing for 2 minutes until they are soft and fragrant but not browned.Toss and finish: Add the blanched, dried beans directly into the skillet with the aromatics. Season with the salt and black pepper.

Toss frequently for 2 to 3 minutes until the beans are heated completely through and coated in the garlic butter. Remove from heat, stir in the lemon juice, top with toasted almonds if using, and serve hot.

title: Garlic Mashed Potato

ingredients.name: whole head of garlic (top sliced off to expose cloves)

steps: Roast the garlic: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Drizzle the exposed cloves of the garlic head with olive oil and wrap tightly in aluminum foil.

Roast in the oven for 30 minutes until the cloves are completely soft and caramelized. Squeeze the soft garlic cloves out of their skins into a small bowl and mash into a smooth paste.Boil the potatoes: While the garlic roasts, place the potato chunks into a large pot and cover with cold water by an inch.

Add a generous tablespoon of salt. Bring to a rolling boil over medium-high heat and cook for 15 to 20 minutes until the potatoes are completely fork-tender.Drain and dry: Drain the potatoes thoroughly in a colander.

Return the drained potatoes to the hot, empty pot over low heat for 1 minute, shaking gently, to steam off any excess moisture. This step ensures your mashed potatoes will not be watery.Mash and incorporate: Press the warm potatoes through a potato ricer or mash them thoroughly with a hand masher.
Stir in the roasted garlic paste, melted butter, and warm milk or heavy cream. Season with the salt and black pepper, whipping gently with a wooden spoon until smooth and creamy.
Transfer to a serving bowl and garnish with fresh chives.

title: CLASSIC DEVILLED EGGS WITH BABY GREENS

ingredients.name: large eggs (preferably a few days old, which makes peeling easier)
fresh baby greens (such as baby spinach, arugula, and mizuna blend)

steps: Boil the eggs: Place the eggs in a single layer in a medium saucepan and cover them with cold water by an inch. Bring to a rolling boil over high heat, then immediately cover the pot with a lid and remove it from the heat source.

Let the eggs sit undisturbed in the hot water for exactly 12 minutes.Chill and peel: While the eggs sit, prepare a large bowl filled with cold water and ice cubes. Transfer the cooked eggs immediately into the ice bath with a slotted spoon and let them cool completely for 15 minutes.

Gently tap the eggs on a hard surface to crack the shells, then peel them under cool running water and pat completely dry.Mix the filling: Slice each peeled egg in half lengthwise. Carefully scoop out the hard-cooked egg yolks into a medium bowl, and place the empty white halves face-up on a clean plate.

Mash the yolks with a fork until fine and crumbly. Add the mayonnaise, Dijon mustard, apple cider vinegar, salt, black pepper, and garlic powder, stirring vigorously until the filling is perfectly smooth and creamy.Assemble and serve: Toss the baby greens with the olive oil, lemon juice, and a tiny pinch of salt in a bowl.
Arrange the dressed greens across a serving platter to form a bed. Spoon or pipe the egg yolk mixture evenly back into the cavities of the egg whites.
Place the stuffed eggs onto the bed of greens, dust the tops lightly with paprika, garnish with chopped chives, and serve chilled.

title: CARAMELIZED ONION TART

ingredients.name: store-bought sheet puff pastry (thawed but kept cold)
large egg (beaten with 1 tbsp water for egg wash)
(approx. 115 g) soft goat cheese or shredded Gruyère

steps: Caramellize the onions: Melt the butter and olive oil together in a large skillet over medium-low heat. Add the sliced onions, thyme, salt, and black pepper.

Cook slowly for 35 to 40 minutes, stirring every few minutes, until the onions are deeply golden brown and sweet. Stir in the balsamic vinegar and brown sugar, cooking for 2 more minutes until the liquid absorbs, then remove from heat to cool slightly.Preheat and prep pastry: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.

Roll the cold puff pastry sheet out onto the parchment into a large rectangle. Use a sharp knife to gently score a 1/2-inch border around the entire edge—do not cut all the way through, as this border will rise to form the crust.Assemble the tart: Poke the inner rectangle of the pastry all over with a fork to prevent it from puffing up too much.
In a small bowl, mix the cheese with the heavy cream until spreadable, then spread it evenly across the inner rectangle. Layer the cooled caramelized onions smoothly on top of the cheese base.

Brush the outer 1/2-inch scored pastry border with the egg wash.Bake and serve: Bake in the preheated oven for 15 to 20 minutes until the puff pastry edges are high, crisp, and a deep golden brown. Remove from the oven, let cool for 5 minutes, then slice into squares and serve warm or at room temperature.

title: ROASTED ZUCCHINI

ingredients.name: and sliced into 1/2-inch thick rounds or spears

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 425°F (220°C). Slice the washed and trimmed zucchinis into uniform ½-inch thick rounds or lengthwise spears so they roast evenly without becoming mushy.Toss with oil and spices: Place the zucchini slices directly onto a large rimmed baking sheet.

Drizzle with the olive oil, then add the minced garlic, dried Italian seasoning, salt, and black pepper. Toss thoroughly with your hands or tongs until every piece is uniformly coated.Arrange and roast: Spread the zucchini out into a single flat layer, ensuring the pieces do not overlap so they roast and brown instead of steaming.

Roast in the preheated oven for 15 to 20 minutes, flipping them halfway through, until the edges are beautifully caramelized and the center is tender.Finish and serve: Remove the baking sheet from the oven. Transfer the hot roasted zucchini to a serving platter, top immediately with the grated Parmesan cheese and fresh chopped parsley, and serve hot.

title: BUTTERED HERB NOODLE

ingredients.name: kosher salt (plus more for boiling water)

steps: Boil the noodles: Bring a large pot of water to a rolling boil over high heat. Add a generous tablespoon of kosher salt.

Drop in the wide egg noodles and cook for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until they are perfectly al dente.Reserve water and drain: Scoop out about ¼ cup of the starchy pasta water from the pot and set it aside. Drain the noodles thoroughly in a colander, but do not rinse them.Melt butter and toss: Return the hot, empty pot to low heat.

Melt the butter until it is foaming, then add the garlic powder, salt, and black pepper. Immediately add the drained noodles back into the pot, tossing gently to coat them completely in the seasoned butter.Incorporate herbs and serve: Pour in the fresh parsley and chives.
If the noodles look dry, add 1 to 2 tablespoons of the reserved pasta water to create a glossy, smooth butter sauce. Toss one final time, transfer to a serving dish, and serve hot.

title: SAUTEED SHRIMP AND PESTO PASTA

ingredients.name: large shrimp (peeled, deveined, tails removed, patted dry)

steps: Boil the pasta: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Cook the pasta according to package instructions until al dente.

Reserve ½ cup of the starchy pasta water, then drain the pasta and set it aside.Sear the shrimp: While the pasta cooks, heat 1 tbsp of olive oil and 1 tbsp of butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season the dry shrimp with salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes.

Add the shrimp to the hot skillet in a single layer and sear for 2 minutes without moving them.Flip and aromatic toss: Flip the shrimp over and add the minced garlic and halved cherry tomatoes if using. Sauté for another 1 to 2 minutes until the garlic is fragrant and the shrimp are pink, opaque, and cooked through.

Remove the skillet from the heat immediately so the shrimp do not become rubbery.Combine and emulsify: Add the hot, drained pasta, the remaining 1 tbsp of olive oil, and the basil pesto directly into the skillet with the shrimp. Toss everything together gently.
If the sauce is too thick, splash in 2 to 3 tablespoons of the reserved pasta water to loosen it up. Top with the grated Parmesan cheese and serve hot.

title: SALMON FILET WITH TZATZIKI SAUCE

ingredients.name: freshly cracked black pepperFinish: Lemon wedges and extra dill for garnish

steps: Make the tzatziki: Place the grated cucumber into a fine-mesh strainer or clean kitchen towel and squeeze hard to remove excess moisture. Transfer the dry cucumber to a medium bowl.

Stir in the Greek yogurt, minced garlic, lemon juice, olive oil, chopped dill, and salt until smooth. Cover and refrigerate.Prep the fish: Pat the salmon filets completely dry on both sides with paper towels.

Rub the flesh side of the fish evenly with the olive oil. Season the filets generously with the garlic powder, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper.Sear the salmon: Heat a large skillet (preferably cast iron) over medium-high heat.
Place the salmon filets skin-side down in the hot pan. Press down gently with a spatula for 10 seconds.

Sear undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes until the skin is very crispy and the flesh turns opaque halfway up the side.Flip and serve: Carefully flip the filets over to the flesh side. Cook for an additional 2 to 3 minutes until the inside flakes easily and is cooked to medium.
Remove the salmon from the skillet, transfer to serving plates, and top each filet with a generous dollop of the chilled tzatziki sauce.

title: SAUTEED BABY PEPPERS

ingredients.name: washed and left whole (or halved lengthwise and seeded)

steps: Prep the peppers: Wash the baby peppers thoroughly and pat them completely dry. You can leave them whole with the stems attached for an attractive presentation, or slice them in half lengthwise and remove the seeds if you prefer easier eating.Heat the pan: Heat the olive oil and melt the butter together in a large skillet over medium-high heat until hot and bubbling.Sauté and blister: Add the baby peppers to the skillet in a single layer.

Sauté for 6 to 8 minutes, tossing only occasionally, so the skins have time to blister, char, and soften slightly.Season and finish: Turn the heat down to medium and stir in the garlic, salt, and black pepper. Sauté for 1 to 2 minutes longer until the garlic is highly fragrant.
Remove from the heat, drizzle immediately with the balsamic vinegar, toss with fresh parsley, and serve hot.

title: STICKY JASMINE RICE
steps: Soak and rinse: Place the jasmine rice into a medium pot or bowl. Cover with cold water, swirl with your fingers, and drain.

Repeat 3 to 4 times until the water runs clear to remove excess surface starch. Cover with fresh water and let soak for 30 minutes to help the grains expand and stick together, then drain completely.Bring to a boil: Combine the soaked and drained jasmine rice, 2 cups of water, optional coconut oil or butter, and salt in a medium heavy-bottomed saucepan.

Bring the mixture to a rolling boil over high heat, stirring once to prevent sticking.Simmer and cover: As soon as the water boils, cover the saucepan tightly with a well-fitting lid. Reduce the heat to the lowest setting immediately.

Let simmer undisturbed for 15 minutes—do not lift the lid or stir the rice during this time, as the trapped steam is crucial.Steam and fluff: Remove the saucepan from the heat source but leave the lid securely on. Let the rice steam undisturbed for 10 minutes to finish cooking and allow the starches to set into a sticky consistency.
Remove the lid, fluff gently with a fork or rice paddle, and serve warm.

title: RIBBON SALAD WITH ORANGE VINAIGRETTE
steps: Shave the vegetables: Lay a peeled carrot flat on a cutting board. Hold it by the stem end and use a Y-shaped vegetable peeler to shave long, thin ribbons down the length of the carrot.

Repeat this process with the zucchinis and cucumber, shaving until you reach the watery seed cores (discard the cores or save for snacking).Crisp the ribbons: Transfer all the vegetable ribbons into a large bowl of ice water for 5 minutes. This makes the ribbons curl beautifully and gives them an extra-crispy texture.

Drain thoroughly and pat completely dry with a clean kitchen towel.Whisk the vinaigrette: Combine the fresh orange juice, olive oil, white wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, honey, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl or mason jar. Whisk vigorously until the dressing is completely emulsified and smooth.Toss and serve: Place the dried vegetable ribbons into a large salad bowl.

Drizzle the orange vinaigrette over the top and toss gently with your hands or salad tongs to coat the ribbons without breaking them. Garnish with the toasted sliced almonds, fresh mint leaves, and crumbled cheese if using.
Serve immediately.

title: CREAM OF ASPARAGUS SOUP WITH SOUR CREAM

ingredients.name: tough wooden ends snapped off and discarded
medium leek (white and light green parts only, washed and sliced)
a few reserved asparagus tips for garnish

steps: Prep and reserve tips: Chop the asparagus stalks into 1-inch pieces. Slice off a few of the pretty top tips (about 8 to 10) and set them aside to use as a garnish later.Sauté the aromatics: Melt the butter in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.

Add the sliced leek and minced garlic. Sauté for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring frequently, until the leeks are soft and translucent but not browned.Simmer the soup: Add the chopped asparagus pieces (excluding the reserved tips), chicken or vegetable broth, salt, and black pepper to the pot.

Bring the liquid to a boiling point, then reduce the heat to medium-low. Cover with a lid and simmer gently for 10 to 12 minutes until the asparagus is completely fork-tender.Blend and enrich: Remove the pot from the heat.

Use an immersion blender directly in the pot (or transfer the liquid carefully in batches to a high-speed countertop blender) and purée until completely velvety smooth. Stir in the sour cream and fresh lemon juice until fully incorporated and uniform in color.Garnish and serve: Drop the reserved asparagus tips into a small bowl of boiling water for 1 minute to blanch them, then shock in ice water.
Ladle the hot puréed soup into deep bowls, top each with a small dollop or swirl of extra sour cream, and float the blanched asparagus tips on top.

title: CARROT AND PEA MIX

ingredients.name: medium carrots (peeled and diced into 1/2-inch cubes)
frozen sweet peas (no need to thaw)
low-sodium chicken or vegetable broth (or water)

steps: Sauté and steam carrots: Melt the butter in a medium skillet or saucepan over medium heat. Add the diced carrots and sauté for 2 minutes to coat them in butter.

Pour in the broth or water, bring to a simmer, then cover the skillet with a lid. Cook for 4 to 5 minutes until the carrots are nearly tender.Add the peas: Remove the lid and stir in the frozen sweet peas.

Cook uncovered for 2 to 3 minutes, stirring frequently, until the peas are heated through, vibrant green, and the excess moisture in the pan has evaporated.Season the mix: Stir in the salt, black pepper, and garlic powder, tossing the vegetables thoroughly to distribute the spices evenly.Garnish and serve: Remove the skillet from the heat source. Fold in the fresh chopped dill or parsley, transfer to a serving dish, and serve hot.

title: STEAMED CAULIFLOWER

ingredients.name: melted butter (or extra virgin olive oil)

steps: Prep the cauliflower: Trim the outer green leaves away from the cauliflower head and cut out the thick central core. Break or slice the remaining cauliflower into uniform, bite-sized florets so that they cook at the exact same rate.Set up the steamer: Pour the water into a large pot and place a steamer basket inside, ensuring the water level sits just below the bottom of the basket.

Bring the water to a rolling boil over high heat.Steam the florets: Add the cauliflower florets into the steamer basket in an even layer. Cover the pot tightly with a lid, reduce the heat to medium-high, and steam for 4 to 5 minutes until the florets are tender when pierced with a fork but still hold their shape.Season and serve: Carefully remove the steamer basket from the pot to stop the cooking process.
Transfer the hot cauliflower to a serving bowl, drizzle evenly with the melted butter and lemon juice if using, toss gently with salt and pepper, and serve immediately.

title: CORNBREAD

ingredients.name: (1 stick) unsalted butter (melted and cooled slightly)

steps: Preheat and prep pan: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Grease an 8-inch square baking dish or a 9-inch cast-iron skillet with a teaspoon of butter or vegetable oil and set aside.Whisk dry ingredients: Combine the yellow cornmeal, all-purpose flour, white sugar, baking powder, baking soda, and kosher salt in a large mixing bowl.

Whisk thoroughly to break up any lumps and ensure the leavening agents are evenly distributed.Mix wet ingredients: Whisk the room-temperature buttermilk, beaten eggs, melted unsalted butter, and honey together in a separate medium bowl until completely smooth and uniform.Combine and bake: Pour the wet ingredients directly into the bowl with the dry ingredients. Stir gently with a rubber spatula just until combined and no dry flour pockets remain—do not overmix, or the cornbread will become dense and tough.
Pour the batter into your prepared pan and smooth the top. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes until the top is golden brown and a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.
Let cool for 10 minutes before slicing.

title: SMOKED SALMON & AVOCADO MOUSSE PLATE

ingredients.name: sour cream or plain Greek yogurt

steps: Blend the mousse: Place the ripe avocado flesh, sour cream, fresh lime juice, minced garlic, salt, and cayenne pepper into a blender or food processor. Blend on high speed for 1 to 2 minutes, scraping down the sides as needed, until the mixture is completely smooth, airy, and velvety.Chill to set: Transfer the avocado mousse into a bowl or a piping bag fitted with a large star tip.

Cover tightly, pressing plastic wrap directly onto the surface of the mousse to prevent browning, and refrigerate for at least 1 hour to let it firm up.Dress the greens: Whisk the extra virgin olive oil, fresh lemon juice, and a tiny pinch of salt together in a small bowl. Toss the mixed baby greens lightly in the dressing to coat them, then divide the greens evenly among 4 serving plates to create a fresh base.Assemble and garnish: Artfully arrange the cold-smoked salmon slices on top of the bed of greens.

Pipe or spoon generous dollops of the chilled avocado mousse alongside the salmon. Garnish the plates evenly with the drained capers, minced red onion, fresh dill sprigs, and a final turn of freshly cracked black pepper.
Serve chilled with toasted baguette slices or crackers.

title: ROASTED RED PEPPER SOUP

ingredients.name: (16 oz / 450 g each) roasted red peppers (drained and chopped)
or 4 large fresh red bell peppers (roasted, peeled, and seeded)
heavy cream or full-fat coconut milk (optional, for richness)
a drizzle of extra virgin olive oil

steps: Sauté the aromatics: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the diced onion and cook for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until soft and translucent.

Stir in the minced garlic and dried basil, cooking for an additional 1 minute until highly fragrant.Simmer the soup: Add the chopped roasted red peppers, broth, kosher salt, and black pepper into the pot. Stir well to combine.

Bring the mixture to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce the heat to low, cover with a lid, and simmer gently for 10 to 12 minutes to blend the flavors.Purée until smooth: Remove the pot from the heat. Use an immersion blender directly in the pot (or carefully transfer the hot liquid in batches to a high-speed countertop blender) and blend for 1 to 2 minutes until completely smooth and velvety.Enrich and serve: Stir in the heavy cream or coconut milk if using, and warm the soup through on low heat for 1 minute (do not let it boil).
Ladle the hot soup into bowls, drizzle each portion with a little olive oil, and garnish generously with fresh chopped basil.

title: GRILLED ZUCCHINI CHUNKS

ingredients.name: a squeeze of fresh lemon juice

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your outdoor grill or indoor grill pan to medium-high heat (approx. 400°F / 200°C).

Cut each trimmed zucchini lengthwise into quarters, then slice crosswise into thick 1-inch chunks so they maintain a firm texture on the grates.Coat and season: Place the zucchini chunks into a large mixing bowl. Drizzle with the extra virgin olive oil, then add the minced garlic, dried Italian seasoning, kosher salt, and freshly cracked black pepper.

Toss thoroughly until every chunk is completely and uniformly coated.Grill the chunks: Thread the zucchini chunks onto metal or soaked wooden skewers to prevent them from slipping through the grates (or use a heavy-duty grill basket). Place them directly onto the hot grates.

Grill for 4 to 5 minutes per side, turning once, until beautiful char marks develop and the center is tender.Garnish and serve: Remove the zucchini from the grill and slide the chunks off the skewers onto a serving platter. Drizzle immediately with a fresh squeeze of lemon juice to brighten the flavors, then top with the grated Parmesan cheese and chopped fresh parsley.
Serve hot.

title: STEAMED CARROT WEDGES

ingredients.name: peeled and cut lengthwise into thick wedges
fresh thyme leaves or chopped parsley

steps: Prep the carrots: Peel the carrots and trim off the top and bottom ends. Cut them in half crosswise to shorten them, then slice each section lengthwise into uniform, thick wedges so that they steam at the exact same rate.Set up the steamer: Pour the water into a medium pot and place a steamer basket inside, making sure the water level sits just below the bottom of the basket.

Bring the water to a rolling boil over high heat.Steam the wedges: Add the carrot wedges into the steamer basket and spread them out evenly. Cover the pot tightly with a lid, reduce the heat to medium-high, and steam for 6 to 8 minutes until the wedges are tender when pierced with a fork but still hold their shape.Season and serve: Carefully lift the steamer basket out of the pot to stop the cooking process.
Transfer the hot carrots to a serving bowl. Toss immediately with the butter, salt, black pepper, and fresh herbs until the butter melts completely into a light glaze.
Serve hot.

title: SHOOFLY PIE

ingredients.name: store-bought 9-inch unbaked deep-dish pie crust (or homemade pastry dough)

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Place your unbaked pie crust into a 9-inch pie dish, crimp the edges neatly, and keep it in the refrigerator so the pastry stays cold while you prepare the fillings.Make the crumbs: Combine the all-purpose flour, brown sugar, ground cinnamon, ground nutmeg, and salt in a medium bowl.

Add the cold cubed butter. Use your fingers or a pastry cutter to rub the butter into the flour mixture until it forms a uniform, sandy texture with small, pea-sized crumbs.Mix the liquid: Dissolve the baking soda into the boiling water in a separate heatproof bowl or large measuring cup.

Whisk in the dark molasses until fully combined, then let the mixture cool slightly for 2 minutes before whisking in the beaten egg until perfectly smooth.Layer and bake: Assemble the pie using the traditional layered method. Pour one-third of the molasses liquid into the bottom of the chilled pie crust, then sprinkle one-third of the crumbs over it.

Repeat this layering twice more, ending with a thick, even layer of crumbs on top. Bake at 400°F (200°C) for 15 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 350°F (175°C) and bake for another 30 to 35 minutes until the center is set and the crumb topping is beautifully golden brown.
Let cool completely on a wire rack before slicing so the gooey bottom layer firms up.

title: GRILLED EGGPLANT CAPRESE DISH

ingredients.name: washed and cut into 1/2-inch thick rounds
large ripe beefsteak tomatoes (sliced into rounds)
fresh basil leaves (whole or thinly sliced)

steps: Prep and salt eggplant: Cut the washed eggplants into uniform ½-inch thick rounds. Arrange them on a baking sheet lined with paper towels and sprinkle lightly with a pinch of salt.

Let them sit for 10 minutes to sweat out excess moisture, then thoroughly pat both sides completely dry.Preheat and season: Preheat your outdoor grill or indoor grill pan to medium-high heat (approx. 400°F / 200°C).

Brush both sides of the dried eggplant rounds evenly with the 3 tbsp of olive oil, and season them with the salt and black pepper.Grill the rounds: Place the eggplant slices directly onto the hot, clean grill grates. Grill undisturbed for 3 to 4 minutes per side until beautiful dark char marks develop and the center of the eggplant becomes tender.

Remove from the heat.Stack and serve: Immediately rub the hot grilled eggplant slices with the cut side of the garlic clove for a subtle aroma. Assemble the Caprese stacks on a large serving platter by layering a warm grilled eggplant round, a tomato slice, a fresh mozzarella slice, and a fresh basil leaf.
Repeat the layers, then finish with a final drizzle of balsamic glaze and extra virgin olive oil. Serve warm.

title: BBQ PORK BACK RIBS

ingredients.name: racks pork back ribs (approx. 3-4 lbs total, membrane removed)
to 1 1/2 cups of your favourite BBQ sauce

steps: Trim and rub: Preheat your combi oven to 320°F (160°C) set to 25% humidity. Pat the pork back ribs completely dry with paper towels.

Mix the brown sugar, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, black pepper, and chili powder together in a small bowl. Rub the spice mixture generously all over both sides of the ribs, pressing it firmly into the meat.Wrap and seal: Lay out a large sheet of foil for each rack, top it with a sheet of parchment paper, and place a rack of ribs on top.

Place 2 tsp of butter on each rack of ribs. Wrap the ribs tightly first in the parchment paper, and then securely in the aluminum foil to trap all the moisture and juices.Combi steam bake: Place the wrapped packets bone-side down on a baking sheet.

Cook in the combi oven at 320°F (160°C) with 25% humidity for 2½ hours until the meat is incredibly tender and starts pulling away from the bone. Remove from the oven and let the ribs rest, completely wrapped, for 10 to 15 minutes.Sauce and glaze: Increase the oven temperature to 350°F (175°C) and turn off the steam setting.
Carefully unwrap the ribs and place them back on the baking sheet. Brush both sides generously with a thick layer of BBQ sauce.

Bake uncovered for exactly 7 minutes to caramelize the sauce into a sticky glaze. Remove from the oven and immediately tent tightly with foil to keep covered during service to avoid drying out the meat.

title: STEAMED GREEN BEANS

ingredients.name: washed and tough stem ends snapped or trimmed off

steps: Prep the green beans: Wash the green beans thoroughly under cold running water. Line up a handful at a time and slice off the tough stem ends, leaving the curly tails intact if desired for a rustic look.Set up the steamer: Pour the water into a large pot and place a steamer basket inside, ensuring the water level sits completely below the bottom of the basket.

Cover the pot and bring the water to a rolling boil over high heat.Steam the beans: Add the trimmed green beans into the steamer basket in an even layer. Cover the pot tightly with a lid, reduce the heat to medium-high, and steam for 4 to 5 minutes until the beans turn a vibrant bright green and are crisp-tender.Season and serve: Carefully lift the steamer basket out of the pot to halt the steaming process.
Transfer the hot green beans immediately to a serving bowl, toss with the butter, salt, black pepper, and fresh lemon juice if using until the butter melts into a light glaze, and serve hot.

title: ROASTED ROOT VEGETABLE

ingredients.name: small celeriac (celery root, peeled and diced)
large beetroot (all peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks)

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C). Peel the sweet potato, carrots, parsnip, beetroot, and celeriac.

Cut all the vegetables into uniform 1-inch chunks so that they roast at the exact same rate.Coat and season: Place all the chopped root vegetables into a large mixing bowl. Drizzle generously with the extra virgin olive oil, then add the minced garlic, chopped rosemary, thyme leaves, kosher salt, and black pepper.

Toss thoroughly until every chunk is completely and evenly coated.Spread and roast: Spread the seasoned vegetables out into a single flat layer on a large rimmed baking sheet. Ensure they are not crowded together so they roast and caramelize instead of steaming.

Roast in the preheated oven for 30 to 35 minutes, tossing them halfway through, until they are fork-tender and beautifully browned at the edges.Garnish and serve: Remove from the oven and transfer the hot roasted vegetables to a serving platter. Garnish with fresh chopped parsley and a light drizzle of balsamic glaze if using, then serve hot.

title: TOMATO PUFF TART

ingredients.name: sheet store-bought puff pastry (thawed but kept cold)
large egg (beaten with 1 tbsp water for egg wash)
large ripe tomatoes (such as Roma or beefsteak, sliced into 1/4-inch rounds)

steps: Drain the tomatoes: Arrange the tomato slices in a single layer on top of several sheets of paper towels. Sprinkle them lightly with the kosher salt.

Let them sit undisturbed for 15 minutes to draw out excess moisture, then thoroughly pat the tops dry with more paper towels to prevent a soggy pastry crust.Preheat and prep pastry: Preheat your oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper. Roll the cold puff pastry sheet out onto the parchment.

Use a sharp knife to gently score a ½-inch border around the entire edge—do not cut all the way through, as this border will rise to form the crust. Poke the inner rectangle all over with a fork to prevent it from puffing up too much.Assemble the tart: In a small bowl, mix the soft cheese, olive oil, and minced garlic together until smooth and spreadable.
Spread this mixture evenly across the inner rectangle of the pastry. Arrange the dried tomato slices over the cheese base in overlapping rows.

Sprinkle the fresh thyme leaves and black pepper over the tomatoes. Brush the outer ½-inch pastry border evenly with the egg wash.Bake and serve: Bake in the preheated oven for 20 to 25 minutes until the puff pastry edges are puffed, crisp, and deeply golden brown.
Remove from the oven, let cool for 5 minutes, then scatter the fresh sliced basil on top and drizzle with balsamic glaze if using. Slice and serve warm.

title: JAMBALAYA
steps: Brown meats: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown sliced sausage and chicken thighs for 5 to 6 minutes; remove and set aside.
Sauté holy trinity: Add onion, bell pepper, and celery to the remaining fat. Sauté for 5 minutes until soft, then stir in minced garlic and Cajun seasoning for 1 minute.

Simmer rice: Pour in crushed tomatoes, chicken broth, and add the bay leaf. Stir in the uncooked rice, then return the sausage and chicken to the pot. Bring to a boil, cover tightly, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 20 minutes.

Add shrimp and finish: Remove lid, tuck the shrimp into the top of the rice, cover again, and cook for 5 minutes until the shrimp are pink and rice is tender. Discard bay leaf, fluff with a fork, and garnish with green onions.

title: CHICKEN BROCCOLI RICE CASSEROLE
steps: Preheat and blanch: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and grease a 9x13 baking dish. Steam or blanch broccoli florets for 2 minutes until bright green; drain well.

Build cream sauce: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Sauté onion for 3 minutes, whisk in flour for 1 minute, then slowly whisk in milk and chicken broth. Simmer for 3 minutes until thickened. Stir in garlic powder, salt, pepper, and 1.5 cups of cheddar until melted.
Combine and layer: Stir the cooked rice, shredded chicken, and blanched broccoli directly into the cheese sauce until fully coated. Pour the mixture into the prepared baking dish.
Bake golden: Sprinkle the remaining ½ cup of cheddar cheese evenly over the top. Bake uncovered for 20 minutes until bubbling and golden-brown on top.

title: ROASTED BROCCOLINI

ingredients.name: bunches fresh broccolini (ends trimmed, thick stalks split lengthwise)

steps: Preheat and prep: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Trim the dry bottom ends of the broccolini stalks and slice thick stems down the middle lengthwise.
Season stalks: Place broccolini directly onto a large rimmed baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil, then scatter garlic slices, salt, black pepper, and red pepper flakes over them.
Spread out: Toss well to coat, then spread into a single flat layer so they roast and crisp up instead of steaming.
Roast and brighten: Roast for 12 to 15 minutes until the florets are charred and crispy and the stems are tender. Remove from oven, drizzle with lemon juice, and serve.

title: ROASTED POTATO

ingredients.name: Russet or Yukon Gold potatoes (peeled and cut into 1-inch cubes)

steps: Preheat and dry: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Cut peeled potatoes into uniform 1-inch cubes and pat completely dry with a kitchen towel.
Toss seasonings: Place potato cubes in a large bowl, drizzle with olive oil, and toss with garlic powder, dried herbs, salt, and black pepper until fully coated.
Arrange on pan: Spread the potatoes out in a single flat layer on a large rimmed baking sheet, leaving space between chunks.
Roast crisp: Roast for 30 to 35 minutes, flipping them halfway through, until the outsides are deeply golden-brown and crispy.

title: STRAWBERRY LEMONADE BARS
steps: Bake crust base: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line an 8-inch square baking pan with parchment. Stir melted butter, sugar, flour, and salt together into a soft dough. Press firmly into the bottom of the pan and bake 15 minutes until lightly golden.
Whisk filling: While crust bakes, whisk granulated sugar and 3 tbsp flour together. Whisk in the eggs one at a time, followed by lemon juice, lemon zest, and smooth strawberry puree.
Pour and bake: Pour the pink strawberry lemon filling directly over the hot, baked crust. Lower oven heat to 325°F (160°C).
Set and chill: Bake for 18 to 20 minutes until the center is set and no longer jiggles. Cool to room temperature, then refrigerate for 2 hours before slicing into bars.

title: LEMON ORZO SALAD

ingredients.name: juice and zest of 1 large lemon

steps: Boil orzo: Cook orzo pasta in boiling salted water according to package instructions (about 8 to 9 minutes); drain and rinse with cold water to cool.
Whisk dressing: Whisk extra-virgin olive oil, lemon juice, lemon zest, minced garlic, chopped parsley, salt, and black pepper together in a small bowl.
Assemble salad: Combine cooled orzo, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, and diced red onion in a large serving bowl.
Dress and garnish: Pour vinaigrette over salad base, toss thoroughly, scatter crumbled feta cheese over top, and serve chilled.

title: TARRAGON CHICKEN SALAD CROSTINI
steps: Bake crostini: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Arrange baguette rounds on a baking sheet, brush with olive oil, and bake 6 to 8 minutes until golden and crispy.
Mix chicken salad: Combine diced chicken breast, mayonnaise, fresh lemon juice, diced celery, chopped fresh tarragon, salt, and black pepper in a medium bowl.
Stir smooth: Mix the chicken salad thoroughly using a spoon until completely integrated and uniform in texture.
Assemble slices: Spoon a generous mound of the creamy tarragon chicken salad onto each crispy, warm crostini toast round and serve immediately.

title: SAUTEED PEPPERS

ingredients.name: large bell peppers (red, yellow, orange, cored and cut into 1/4-inch thin strips)

steps: Heat skillet: Heat the olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat until hot.
Sauté strips: Add the thin bell pepper strips and sliced yellow onion to the pan. Sauté for 5 to 6 minutes, stirring frequently, until they soften and the edges turn slightly golden.
Add garlic: Turn heat down to medium, add sliced garlic, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper, tossing constantly for 1 to 2 minutes until garlic is highly fragrant.
Finish splash: Drizzle with red wine vinegar, toss one final time, remove from heat, and serve hot.

title: SPANISH RICE
steps: Toast grains: Heat vegetable oil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat. Add white rice and cook, stirring constantly, for 3 to 4 minutes until grains are opaque and golden-brown.
Sauté aromatics: Lower heat to medium. Add finely diced onion and minced garlic, stirring continuously for 1 to 2 minutes until soft and fragrant.
Simmer base liquid: Pour in tomato sauce, chicken broth, chili powder, cumin, and salt, stirring well to combine. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil.

Steam covered: Cover tightly with a lid and drop heat to ultra-low. Simmer undisturbed for 15 to 18 minutes until liquid is fully absorbed. Remove from heat, let sit covered for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork.

title: SMOTHERED PORK CHOPS IN MUSHROOM GRAVY
steps: Sear chops: Season pork chops with salt and pepper, then dust lightly in 2 tbsp flour. Heat oil and 1 tbsp butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear chops for 4 minutes per side until golden; remove to a plate.
Sauté vegetables: Add remaining 1 tbsp butter to the same pan. Sauté sliced mushrooms and onions for 5 to 6 minutes until soft and deeply browned.

Build gravy: Sprinkle 2 tbsp flour over the mushrooms and onions, stirring for 1 minute. Slowly pour in beef broth and Worcestershire sauce, whisking constantly to scrape up pan bits. Bring to a simmer to thicken.
Smother and serve: Turn heat to low, stir in heavy cream, and return pork chops and juices to the pan. Cover and simmer for 5 minutes until chops are cooked through (145°F) and tender.

title: GRILLED COD WITH ROMESCO SAUCE
steps: Blend Romesco: Place drained roasted red peppers, toasted almonds, garlic, olive oil, red wine vinegar, smoked paprika, and salt into a blender. Process on high for 1 minute until a thick, textured sauce forms; set aside.
Preheat grill: Preheat outdoor grill or indoor grill pan to medium-high heat and oil grates thoroughly.
Season cod: Pat cod fillets dry, rub all sides with olive oil, and season with garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.

Grill fillets: Grill cod undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes on the first side until grill marks set and fish flips easily. Carefully turn and cook for 3 to 4 more minutes until opaque and flaky. Serve hot with a thick smear of Romesco sauce.

title: ROASTED BRUSSEL SPROUT WITH LEMON

ingredients.name: Brussels sprouts (trimmed and cut in half lengthwise)
freshly cracked black pepper Finish: Juice and zest of 1/2 fresh lemon

steps: Preheat pan: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Place your empty baking sheet inside the oven while it heats up; a hot pan guarantees better browning.
Toss sprouts: Toss trimmed and halved Brussels sprouts with olive oil, kosher salt, and black pepper in a medium bowl.
Arrange flat: Carefully remove the hot baking sheet from the oven and spread the sprouts out cut-side down in a single layer.
Roast and brighten: Roast for 18 to 20 minutes until the outer leaves are crispy and the flat bottoms are deeply browned. Toss immediately with fresh lemon juice and zest.

title: SWEET POTATO PUREE

ingredients.name: large sweet potatoes (peeled and chopped into 1-inch chunks)

steps: Boil sweet potatoes: Place sweet potato chunks into a large pot, cover with cold water, and bring to a rolling boil over high heat.
Cook tender: Simmer for 15 minutes until chunks are completely tender and fall apart when pierced with a fork; drain well.
Steam off water: Return drained potatoes to the warm pot off heat for 1 minute to let excess moisture steam away.
Purée smooth: Transfer to a food processor or use an immersion blender. Add melted butter, maple syrup, cinnamon, and salt. Process on high for 1 minute until completely whipped and silky smooth.

title: MARGARITA BARS
steps: Bake pretzel crust: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C) and line an 8-inch square pan with parchment. Stir pretzel crumbs, sugar, and melted butter together. Press firmly into pan and bake 10 minutes until set.
Whisk lime filling: While crust bakes, whisk sweetened condensed milk, egg yolks, fresh lime juice, lime zest, and optional tequila together in a bowl until smooth and slightly thickened.
Pour and bake: Pour the tangy lime filling directly over the warm pretzel crust.
Set and slice: Bake for 15 minutes until the edges are set but the center has a tiny jiggle. Cool, then chill in the refrigerator for 2 hours before slicing into salty-sweet bars.

title: VIDALIA ONION SOUP
steps: Caramelize Vidalias: Melt butter and olive oil in a large deep pot over medium-low heat. Add sliced sweet onions, sugar, and salt. Cook slowly for 25 to 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until onions are a deep golden brown and jammy.
Deglaze pan: Pour in white wine or sherry if using, scraping up pan bits. Simmer for 2 minutes until liquid absorbs.

Simmer broth: Pour beef broth and Worcestershire into the pot, adding the thyme sprigs and bay leaf. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes; discard herbs and bay leaf.

Melt cheese top: Preheat oven broiler. Ladle soup into heat-safe bowls, place a toasted baguette slice on top of each, cover heavily with shredded cheese, and broil for 2 minutes until bubbling and brown.

title: GRILLED BEEF STEAK WITH GREEN PEPPERCORN SAUCE

ingredients.name: strip loin or ribeye steaks (approx. 8 oz each)
canned green peppercorns in brine (drained and lightly crushed)

steps: Grill steaks: Preheat grill or cast-iron grill pan to high heat. Rub steaks with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Grill for 4 to 5 minutes per side for medium-rare (internal temp 135°F). Transfer to a board to rest.
Sauté shallot: Melt butter in a small skillet over medium heat. Sauté minced shallot for 2 minutes until soft.
Deglaze pan safely: Pour in the brandy or beef broth, scraping the bottom of the skillet. Simmer for 2 minutes until the liquid reduces by half.

Simmer peppercorn sauce: Stir in the heavy cream and crushed green peppercorns. Simmer gently for 3 to 4 minutes until the cream thickens into a rich savory sauce. Slice steaks and spoon sauce over top.

title: SAUTEED SPINACH

ingredients.name: fresh baby spinach leaves (washed and thoroughly dried)

steps: Heat oil and garlic: Heat extra virgin olive oil in a large skillet or wok over medium heat. Add sliced garlic and cook for 1 minute until fragrant but not browned.
Add spinach: Turn heat up to medium-high and add the fresh baby spinach leaves in large handfuls.
Toss to wilt: Use tongs to continuously turn and toss the leaves for 2 minutes until just wilted and bright green.
Season and serve: Remove from heat immediately to prevent wateriness. Toss with kosher salt and black pepper, and serve hot.

title: BUTTER MASHED POTATO

ingredients.name: Yukon Gold potatoes (peeled and cut into 1-inch chunks)

steps: Boil potato chunks: Place peeled potato chunks into a large pot. Cover with cold water by an inch and add a generous tablespoon of salt. Bring to a boil over high heat.
Cook tender: Cook for 12 to 15 minutes until the potatoes are completely tender when pierced with a fork; drain thoroughly in a colander.
Steam dry: Return drained potatoes immediately to the hot, empty pot over low heat for 1 minute, shaking gently to evaporate any leftover surface water.

Mash smooth: Remove from heat. Use a potato masher or ricer to break down potatoes completely. Pour in the melted butter and warm milk, seasoning with salt and pepper. Stir vigorously with a wooden spoon until smooth and velvety.

title: BROCCOLI CHEDDAR SOUP
steps: Sauté onion base: Melt butter in a medium pot over medium heat. Sauté diced onion for 3 to 4 minutes until soft. Whisk in flour and cook constantly for 1 minute.
Simmer soup base: Slowly pour in chicken broth and whole milk, whisking to avoid lumps. Bring to a low simmer.

Cook vegetables tender: Add the finely chopped broccoli, grated carrot, garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cover and simmer gently over medium-low heat for 10 to 12 minutes until broccoli is completely tender.
Melt cheese: Turn off the heat entirely. Add the shredded cheddar cheese one handful at a time, stirring constantly until completely melted, creamy, and smooth; serve hot.

title: GARLIC SAUTEED SHRIMP
steps: Heat skillet: Heat olive oil and melt butter together in a large skillet over medium-high heat until hot and foaming.
Flash fry garlic: Add minced garlic and red pepper flakes to the pan, stirring constantly for 30 seconds until highly fragrant.
Cook shrimp: Add shrimp in a single layer, season with salt, and sauté 2 minutes per side until they turn pink and opaque.
Brighten and serve: Squeeze fresh lemon juice over the shrimp, toss together, remove from heat, and serve immediately.

title: SEARED GREEN BEANS

ingredients.name: fresh green beans (trimmed and dried thoroughly)

steps: Heat pan hot: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over high heat until shimmering.
Sear undisturbed: Add green beans in a single flat layer. Let sear undisturbed for 2 to 3 minutes until blister marks form on the bottom.
Toss and season: Toss beans, turn heat to medium, and add minced garlic, salt, and black pepper. Sauté for 2 to 3 more minutes until crisp-tender; serve hot.

title: ROASTED ACORN SQUASH WEDGES

ingredients.name: large acorn squash (washed, halved, seeded, and cut into 1-inch thick wedges)

steps: Preheat oven: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a large baking sheet with parchment paper.
Toss wedges: Place acorn squash wedges in a bowl, drizzle with olive oil, and toss with brown sugar, cinnamon, and salt.
Arrange pan: Spread wedges out in a flat single layer on the baking sheet.
Bake caramelized: Roast for 25 to 30 minutes, flipping halfway through, until the squash flesh is completely fork-tender and edges are caramelized.

title: SUMMER MINESTRONE

ingredients.name: green beans, trimmed and cut into 1-inch pieces
100 oz cannellini beans, drained and rinsed

steps: Heat the olive oil in a steam-jacketed kettle or heavy stockpot over medium heat. Add the onions, carrots, and celery; cook for 8 to 10 minutes until softened without browning.
Add the minced garlic and Italian seasoning. Cook for 1 minute, stirring continuously.
Add the zucchini, green beans, diced tomatoes, and vegetable broth. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a steady simmer.
Simmer for 15 minutes, or until the carrots and green beans are nearly tender.
Stir in the cannellini beans and shell pasta. Simmer for 10 to 12 minutes, stirring regularly, until the pasta is tender and the soup reaches at least 165°F (74°C).
Fold in the chopped spinach and cook for 2 minutes until wilted. Season with kosher salt and black pepper, then finish with chopped parsley.
Serve in 8-ounce portions. For hot holding, maintain at 140°F (60°C) or above and stir periodically to keep ingredients evenly distributed.
notes: Add extra hot vegetable broth during holding if the pasta thickens the soup.

title: SALISBURY STEAK IN ONION GRAVY
steps: Shape patties: Mix ground beef, breadcrumbs, egg, Worcestershire sauce, salt, and pepper in a bowl. Shape into 4 oval patties.
Sear meat: Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear patties for 3 to 4 minutes per side until browned; remove to a plate.
Sauté onions: Add butter and sliced onions to the same pan. Sauté over medium heat for 6 minutes until soft and golden brown.
Thicken gravy: Sprinkle flour over onions, stir 1 minute. Slowly pour in beef broth and Worcestershire, whisking constantly. Simmer 3 minutes until thickened, return patties, and simmer 5 minutes.

title: OVEN BAKED BBQ BONE-IN CHICKEN
steps: Preheat and rub: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and line a baking pan with foil. Rub chicken pieces with oil, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and pepper.
Bake skin-side up: Place chicken pieces skin-side up in the pan. Bake uncovered for 30 minutes to cook through and crisp skin.
Sauce coating: Remove pan from oven and brush a thick layer of BBQ sauce heavily over all sides of the chicken pieces.
Glaze finish: Return to oven and bake for an additional 10 to 15 minutes until the BBQ sauce is caramelized and internal chicken temperature hits 165°F (74°C).

title: RATATOUILLE COINS

ingredients.name: roma tomatoes (all sliced into uniform 1/4-inch thick rounds)

steps: Preheat and base: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Spread 1 cup marinara sauce and minced garlic across the bottom of an 8-inch round baking dish.
Arrange coins: Alternating vegetables, pack the eggplant, squash, zucchini, and tomato coins tightly in an overlapping circular pattern over the sauce.
Season top: Drizzle the vegetable coins with olive oil and sprinkle evenly with dried Italian seasoning, salt, and black pepper.
Bake tender: Cover dish with parchment paper and bake for 30 to 35 minutes until vegetables are tender and sauce is bubbling around edges.

title: TRI COLOR CAULIFLOWER GRATIN

ingredients.name: mixed tri-color cauliflower florets (white, purple, orange)

steps: Blanch cauliflower: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C) and grease a 9x13 dish. Steam or boil the tri-color cauliflower florets for 3 minutes until crisp-tender; drain thoroughly.
Build the roux: Melt butter in a saucepan over medium heat. Whisk in flour and cook constantly for 1 minute to remove raw flour taste.
Thicken cream: Slowly whisk in warm milk until smooth. Simmer for 3 to 4 minutes until thick. Remove from heat, stir in nutmeg, salt, pepper, Gruyère, and half the Parmesan until melted.
Layer and bake: Arrange cauliflower in the dish, pour the warm cheese sauce over top, and scatter remaining Parmesan and Panko. Bake uncovered for 20 minutes until bubbling and golden-brown.

title: APEROL SPRITZ TRIFLE

ingredients.name: pre-baked vanilla sponge cake (cut into 1-inch cubes)

steps: Soak sponge cubes: Combine Prosecco, ¼ cup Aperol, and orange juice in a bowl. Arrange sponge cake cubes in the bottom of a large trifle dish and drizzle the alcohol mixture evenly over them.
Layer custard: Fold the fresh orange zest into the vanilla pastry cream, then spread it smoothly over the soaked cake layer.
Whip Aperol cream: Whip heavy cream, powdered sugar, and 2 tbsp Aperol together in a chilled bowl until sturdy, fluffy peaks form.
Assemble and chill: Pipe or spoon the Aperol whipped cream over the custard layer. Garnish beautifully with orange segments and fresh mint leaves, then refrigerate for 3 hours before serving cold.

title: CHICKEN GNOCCHI SOUP
steps: Sauté base: Heat olive oil and melt butter in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté onion, celery, and shredded carrots for 5 minutes until soft; add garlic for 30 seconds.
Simmer broth: Pour in the chicken broth and heavy cream, bringing the mixture to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat.
Cook gnocchi: Drop the potato gnocchi directly into the simmering soup base. Cook uncovered for 4 to 5 minutes until the gnocchi float to the surface.
Finish soup: Fold in the shredded chicken and baby spinach. Simmer for 2 minutes until the spinach wilts completely and chicken is hot. Season with salt and pepper, then serve.

title: CLASSIC POTATO SALAD

ingredients.name: Yukon Gold potatoes (peeled and cut into 3/4-inch cubes)

steps: Boil potato cubes: Place potato cubes in a pot, cover with cold salted water, bring to a boil, and cook 12 to 15 minutes until fork-tender; drain and cool completely.
Whisk dressing: Combine mayonnaise, yellow mustard, apple cider vinegar, salt, garlic powder, and paprika in a small bowl until smooth.
Assemble bowl: Place cooled potatoes, chopped hard-boiled eggs, diced celery, diced red onion, and sweet pickle relish into a large bowl.
Coat and chill: Pour dressing over salad, toss thoroughly to coat, top with a light dust of extra paprika, and refrigerate for 1 hour before serving.

title: LOBSTER TAIL THERMIDOR
steps: Prep tails: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Cut down the top shell of lobster tails, lift meat out to rest on top of shell, brush with oil, and bake 10 minutes until opaque; chop meat into bite-sized pieces, keeping shells intact.

Build sauce: Melt butter in a skillet over medium heat. Sauté shallot and garlic for 2 minutes, pour in white wine, and simmer until reduced by half. Whisk in heavy cream and Dijon, simmering 3 minutes until thick.
Melt cheese: Remove sauce from heat, stir in chopped tarragon, lemon juice, ¼ cup Gruyère, and fold in the chopped lobster meat.
Broil golden: Stuff the creamy lobster mixture back into the empty shells. Top with remaining Gruyère and broil in the oven for 2 minutes until bubbling and golden-brown.

title: PARMESAN GREEN BEANS
steps: Blanch green beans: Cook green beans in a pot of boiling salted water for 3 minutes, then instantly plunge into ice water; drain and pat dry.
Sauté aromatics: Melt butter and olive oil together in a large skillet over medium heat. Sauté minced garlic for 1 minute until fragrant.
Coat beans: Toss the dry green beans into the hot garlic butter skillet, cooking for 2 to 3 minutes until heated completely through.
Cheese finish: Remove pan from heat, season with salt and black pepper, scatter the grated Parmesan cheese evenly over top, toss quickly, and serve hot.

title: CHERRY SLAB PIE BARS

ingredients.name: (21 oz each) high-quality cherry pie filling

steps: Preheat and rub: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and grease a 10x15-inch rimmed baking sheet. Whisk flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt. Rub cold cubed butter into the flour with your fingers until sandy crumbs form; beat in the egg.
Press bottom layer: Press two-thirds of the dough mixture firmly into the bottom of the pan to form an even crust layer.
Spread cherries: Pour the cherry pie filling smoothly over the bottom crust layer using a flat spatula.

Bake crumble: Scatter the remaining one-third of dough crumbs over the cherry layer. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until the top is light golden-brown. Cool, drizzle with a quick whisk of powdered sugar and milk glaze, then slice into squares.

title: MISO & SHRIMIP SOUP WITH SCALLION DUMPLING

ingredients.name: pre -made scallion or vegetable dumplings (frozen is fine)

steps: Heat dashi stock or broth in a large pot over medium heat. Stir in the grated ginger and minced garlic. Bring to a gentle simmer for 5 minutes to infuse the flavors.
Add the sliced shiitake mushrooms and baby bok choy to the simmering broth. Cook for 3 minutes until the vegetables begin to soften.

Drop the scallion dumplings and shrimp directly into the pot. Simmer gently for 4 to 5 minutes until the dumplings are cooked through (floating) and the shrimp turn pink and opaque. Turn off the heat.
Temper the miso paste with hot broth and whisk until completely smooth and lump-free. Pour this mixture back into the pot and stir gently to combine.
Stir in the toasted sesame oil. Ladle the soup evenly into deep bowls, ensuring each portion gets 3 dumplings and plenty of shrimp. Top generously with the sliced fresh scallions.

title: GINGER SOY SALMON WITH JASMINE RICE
steps: Rinse the Jasmine rice thoroughly under cold water until the water runs clear. Combine the rinsed rice, water, and salt in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then cover with a tight lid, reduce heat to low, and simmer for 15 minutes. Remove from heat and let steam covered for 5 minutes before fluffing.
Whisk together the soy sauce, honey, sugar, grated ginger, minced garlic, rice vinegar, and toasted sesame oil until the honey dissolves.
Heat a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Place the salmon fillets skin-side up (flesh-side down) in the pan. Sear undisturbed for 4 minutes until a golden crust forms.
Flip the salmon over. Pour the ginger soy sauce mixture directly into the pan around the fish.

Lower the heat to medium. Simmer for 3 to 4 minutes while continuously spooning the bubbling sauce over the tops of the fillets. The sauce will quickly thicken into a glossy glaze, and the salmon will flake easily when done.
Spoon a generous base of fluffy Jasmine rice into bowls. Place a glazed salmon fillet on top, drizzle with extra pan sauce, and garnish with sliced green onions and sesame seeds.

title: VEGETABLE LASAGNA

ingredients.name: lasagna noodles (oven-ready or boiled al dente)

steps: Sauté the vegetables: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the onion, zucchini, mushrooms, and bell pepper. Sauté for 6 to 8 minutes until tender and their moisture has evaporated. Stir in the garlic and cook for 1 minute. Add the spinach, tossing until completely wilted. Remove from heat.
Stir the ricotta cheese, egg, grated parmesan, dried oregano, salt, and pepper together in a medium bowl until uniform.

Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Spread 1 cup of marinara sauce across the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish. Lay 4 lasagna noodles over the sauce. Spread half of the ricotta mixture over the noodles, top with half of the cooked vegetable filling, drizzle with 1 cup of marinara, and sprinkle with 1 cup of mozzarella.

Add another layer of 4 noodles, the remaining ricotta mixture, the remaining vegetables, another cup of sauce, and 1 cup of mozzarella. Top with the final 4 noodles, the remaining marinara sauce, and the final cup of mozzarella cheese.

Cover the baking dish tightly with aluminum foil (foil should not touch the cheese). Bake for 25 minutes. Remove the foil and bake uncovered for an additional 15 to 20 minutes until the cheese is bubbly and golden brown.
Let the lasagna rest undisturbed for 15 minutes before slicing so the layers hold their shape.

title: MEDITERRANEAN TOMATO, OLIVE, & FETA IN GREEK DRESSING
steps: Combine the extra-virgin olive oil, red wine vinegar, minced garlic, dried oregano, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper in a small bowl or jar. Whisk vigorously or shake well until the dressing emulsifies.
Core and chop the tomatoes into bite-sized chunks. Slice the cucumber and thinly slice the red onion. Place the chopped tomatoes, cucumbers, and onions into a large mixing bowl.
Toss the halved Kalamata olives into the bowl with the vegetables. Gently fold in the cubed or crumbled feta cheese.
Pour the Greek dressing over the salad components. Use a large spoon or salad tongs to toss gently, ensuring everything is evenly coated without breaking up the feta cheese too much.
Let the salad sit at room temperature for 10 minutes before serving. This allows the tomatoes to release their natural juices and mingle with the vinaigrette.

title: MANGO PUDDING

ingredients.name: fresh mango puree (from about 2-3 sweet, ripe mangoes like Ataulfo)
whole milk (or coconut milk for a tropical twist)

steps: Pour the 1/2 cup of hot water (just below boiling) into a medium bowl. Sprinkle the gelatin powder evenly over the top. Stir vigorously with a fork or small whisk for 1 to 2 minutes until completely dissolved and clear.
Stir the granulated sugar into the warm gelatin mixture until the grains completely dissolve.
In a large bowl or blender, combine the smooth mango puree, milk, and the sweet gelatin mixture. Blend or whisk thoroughly until the color is completely uniform and smooth.
Pour the liquid mixture evenly into dessert bowls, ramekins, or glasses. Chill for at least 3 hours until fully set and firm to a gentle touch.
Just before serving, drizzle a tablespoon of cold evaporated milk over the top of each pudding. Crown with a small pile of fresh mango chunks.

title: ROASTED RED PEPPER WITH DILL YOGURT

ingredients.name: large red bell peppers (halved and seeded)
salt, and black pepperDill Yogurt Swirl: 1/2 cup plain Greek yogurt
fresh lemon juice, a pinch of salt

steps: Place pepper halves cut-side down on a foil-lined baking sheet. Roast under a high oven broil for 15 to 20 minutes until the skins are blackened. Transfer to a bowl, cover with plastic wrap for 10 minutes, then peel off and discard the skins. Roughly chop the flesh.Sauté aromatics: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion and garlic for 5 minutes until soft and translucent.
Add the chopped roasted peppers, vegetable broth, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pot. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 10 minutes.
Use an immersion blender or stand blender to puree the soup until completely smooth and velvety.
In a small bowl, stir together the Greek yogurt, fresh dill, lemon juice, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Ladle the hot soup into bowls. Spoon a generous dollop of the dill yogurt into the center of each bowl and swirl it through the red soup.
aliases: ROASTED RED PEPPER SOUP WITH DILL YOGURT (GF)

title: TURKEY MEDALLION WITH CRANBERRY ORANGE CHUTNEY

ingredients.name: turkey breast tenderloin (cut into 1-inch thick medallions)Turkey Seasoning: 1 tbsp olive oil
salt, and black pepperChutney Base: 2 cups fresh or frozen cranberries
medium orange (zested and juiced)Chutney Sweetener & Spice: 1/3 cup honey or maple syrup

steps: Combine cranberries, orange zest, orange juice, honey, apple cider vinegar, red onion, grated ginger, and cinnamon in a medium saucepan over medium heat.

Bring the chutney mixture to a gentle boil. Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer uncovered for 10 to 12 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the cranberries burst and the sauce thickens into a glossy glaze. Remove from heat.
Pat the turkey medallions dry with paper towels. Rub both sides evenly with olive oil, poultry seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and pepper.
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the turkey medallions to the pan in a single layer. Cook undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes until a golden crust forms.
Flip the medallions over. Reduce heat to medium and cook for another 4 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C) on a meat thermometer. Transfer to a plate to rest for 3 minutes.
Arrange the warm turkey medallions on serving plates and spoon the vibrant cranberry orange chutney generously across the top.

title: LOBSTER / SHRIMP ROLL ON TOASTED BRIOCHE

ingredients.name: cooked lobster meat (chopped) and 1/2 lb cooked shrimp (peeled, deveined, and chopped)The
salt, and black pepper (to taste)The Vessel: 4 top-split brioche hot dog buns

steps: Ensure the cooked lobster and shrimp are thoroughly chilled. Chop them into bite-sized chunks and pat dry with paper towels to prevent a watery dressing.

In a large bowl, whisk together the mayonnaise, fresh lemon juice, Old Bay seasoning, salt, and pepper. Fold in the chopped celery, chives, lobster, and shrimp until evenly coated. Hold chilled while prepping the bread.
Heat a skillet or griddle over medium heat. Spread the softened butter generously onto both outer sides of the top-split brioche buns.
Place the buns in the hot skillet. Toast for 2 minutes per side until the edges are golden brown, crispy, and warm.
Carefully open the warm brioche buns. Spoon the chilled lobster and shrimp salad generously into each toasted roll. Garnish with a tiny pinch of extra chives or Old Bay on top.

title: ARUGULA & PEAR WITH BALSAMIC VINAIGRETTE

ingredients.name: ripe Bosc or Anjou pears (thinly sliced)
toasted walnuts or pecans [1, 3]Balsamic Vinaigrette: 1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
salt, and freshly cracked black pepper (to taste) [1

steps: Combine the extra-virgin olive oil, balsamic vinegar, honey, Dijon mustard, salt, and pepper. Whisk vigorously until emulsified.
If the walnuts are not toasted, toss them in a dry skillet over medium heat for 3 to 4 minutes until fragrant, then let them cool completely.
Place the fresh baby arugula into a large salad bowl.
Arrange the thin pear slices evenly over the arugula bed. Top with the crumbled cheese and cooled toasted walnuts.
Drizzle the balsamic vinaigrette over the salad just before serving, tossing gently to coat the delicate leaves without bruising the pears.

title: LEMON SORBET
steps: Store-bought

title: CHICKEN TORTILLA
steps: Sauté aromatics: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion and minced jalapeño for 5 minutes until soft and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 additional minute until fragrant.
Stir the cumin, chili powder, dried oregano, and salt directly into the onion mixture. Let the spices toast in the dry heat for 30 seconds to fully release their essential oils.
Pour in the chicken broth and crushed tomatoes. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil, then turn the heat down to low, cover the pot, and let it simmer gently for 10 minutes to marry the flavors.
Stir in the shredded chicken, rinsed black beans, and corn. Simmer uncovered for 5 minutes until the fillings are heated completely through.
Turn off the heat and stir in the fresh lemon or lime juice to brighten the rich broth. Taste and add extra salt if desired.

Ladle the hot soup into deep bowls. Pile the toppings generously on top of each portion just before serving, starting with the crunchy tortilla chips, followed by avocado chunks, crumbled cheese, and cilantro.

title: GRILLED FISH WITH SALSA
