steps: Toss together the selected salsa base (diced mango or tomatoes), red bell pepper, red onion, cilantro, and jalapeño. Drizzle with the lime juice, extra-virgin olive oil, and salt. Stir well and set aside at room temperature to let the flavors marry.
Rub both sides of the fish fillets with 1 tablespoon of olive oil. Season evenly with the chili powder, garlic powder, cumin, salt, and black pepper.
Heat the grill or grill pan over medium-high heat. Ensure the grates are completely clean and well-oiled to prevent sticking.

Place the fish fillets on the grill. Cook undisturbed for 3 to 4 minutes per side. The fish is ready when it develops nice grill marks, releases easily from the grates, and flakes easily with a fork (internal temperature of 145°F/63°C).
Transfer the grilled fish fillets to serving plates. Spoon the fresh salsa generously over the top of each fillet, allowing the vibrant juices to pool around the fish.
aliases: GRILLED FISH WITH BLACK BEAN AND CORN SALSA (GF/DF)

title: EGGPLANT PARMESAN

ingredients.name: salt (for sweating)Breading Station: 1/2 cup all-purpose flour
fresh basil leaves (torn)Cooking: Olive oil spray

steps: Lay eggplant rounds on paper towels and sprinkle both sides with salt. Let sit for 20 minutes to draw out bitter moisture. Rinse thoroughly under cold water and pat completely dry.
Set oven to 425°F (220°C) and line two large baking sheets with parchment paper.

Set up three shallow bowls: one with flour, one with beaten eggs, and one mixing the panko crumbs and grated parmesan. Dip each eggplant slice into the flour, then the egg, and finally press firmly into the panko mixture to coat evenly.
Arrange the slices in a single layer on the baking sheets. Spray the tops lightly with olive oil spray. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes, flipping halfway through, until golden-brown and crispy.

In an 8x11-inch baking dish, spread 1/2 cup of marinara sauce. Layer half of the baked eggplant slices over the sauce. Top with half of the remaining marinara, 1 cup of mozzarella, and a few torn basil leaves. Repeat with the remaining eggplant, sauce, mozzarella, and a final dust of parmesan.
Bake at 400°F (204°C) for 15 to 20 minutes until the cheese is melted, bubbly, and golden around the edges. Let rest for 10 minutes before slicing to allow the layers to set.
aliases: EGGPLANT PARMESAN WITH TOMATO SAUCE AND MOZZARELLA

title: CORN & AVOCADO WITH HONEY MUSTARD VINAIGRETTE

ingredients.name: sweet corn (fresh shucked, canned, or thawed frozen)
finely diced red onionHoney Mustard Vinaigrette: 3 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil

steps: In a small bowl or jar, whisk together the olive oil, apple cider vinegar, honey, Dijon mustard, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper until completely smooth and emulsified.
Slice the cherry tomatoes in half and finely dice the red onion. Cube the avocados into bite-sized chunks.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the sweet corn, halved cherry tomatoes, and diced red onion.
Add the avocado chunks to the bowl last so they do not get mashed during mixing.
Pour the honey mustard vinaigrette over the ingredients. Use a rubber spatula or large spoon to gently fold the salad together until everything is evenly coated.
aliases: MIXED GREENS WITH AVOCADO, CORN, AND TOMATO.  CHOICE DRESSING (GF/DF)

title: COCONUT FLAN

ingredients.name: waterThe Custard Base: 1 can (14 oz) sweetened condensed milk

steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Set a 9-inch round cake pan or ramekins inside a larger, high-sided roasting pan.

Combine sugar and water in a heavy saucepan over medium-high heat. Swirl the pan occasionally—do not stir—until the sugar melts and turns a deep amber color (about 6 to 8 minutes). Immediately pour the hot caramel into the bottom of the cake pan, swirling quickly to coat the bottom before it hardens.
In a large bowl or blender, combine the sweetened condensed milk, coconut milk, eggs, vanilla extract, and salt. Blend or whisk until completely smooth and uniform.
Pour the custard mixture through a fine-mesh strainer directly over the hardened caramel in the cake pan to ensure an ultra-smooth texture.

Bring a kettle of water to a boil. Pour the hot water into the outer roasting pan until it reaches halfway up the sides of the flan pan. This water bath (bain-marie) ensures even cooking without curdling.
Bake for 45 to 50 minutes. The flan is ready when the edges are set but the center still jiggles slightly like gelatin.
Remove the flan pan from the water bath and let it cool to room temperature. Cover and refrigerate for at least 4 hours to fully set.

Run a thin knife around the edges of the pan. Place a large rimmed serving plate upside down over the pan, then quickly invert both. The flan will slide out, and the liquid caramel sauce will run down the sides. Top with toasted coconut.

title: TOMATO BASIL
steps: Sauté aromatics: Heat the butter and olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion for 5 minutes until soft and translucent. Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 additional minute until fragrant.

Pour in the canned whole tomatoes with their juices, along with the broth. Use a wooden spoon to gently break apart the whole tomatoes. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 15 minutes.
Remove the pot from the heat source. Add the fresh basil leaves directly into the hot liquid. Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until it is completely smooth and velvety.
Stir in the heavy cream and sugar (if using). Taste the broth and adjust with extra salt and pepper as needed.
Return the pot to low heat for 2 minutes just to warm everything through. Ladle into warm bowls and garnish with extra fresh basil leaves and a tiny drizzle of olive oil.

title: PHILLY CHEESESTEAK
steps: Place ribeye steak in the freezer for 20 to 30 minutes before prepping. This firms up the meat and makes it significantly easier to slice into paper-thin ribbons.Sauté the vegetables: Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or flat-top griddle over medium-high heat. Cook the sliced onion and bell pepper for 6 to 8 minutes until soft and caramelized. Move them to the side of the pan.

Crank the heat up to high. Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the empty side of the pan. Toss in the shaved ribeye, breaking it up with a spatula. Season with garlic powder, salt, and pepper. Cook for 2 to 3 minutes until the meat is browned and no longer pink.
Toss the caramelized onions and peppers directly into the cooked beef. Divide the mixture into 4 oblong piles right in the skillet, matching the rough length of the hoagie rolls.
Lay 2 slices of Provolone cheese evenly over the top of each meat pile. Turn off the heat and let the steam melt the cheese completely (about 1 minute).
Open the hoagie rolls and place them face-down directly over the melted cheese piles. Slide a long spatula underneath the meat and lift, flipping the sandwich right-side up. Serve hot.

title: VEGETABLE & TOFU STIRFRY
steps: Press the block of tofu with paper towels and a heavy object for 15 minutes to remove excess water. Cut into 1-inch cubes.
In a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, water, honey, and cornstarch until the cornstarch completely dissolves. Set aside.

Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the tofu cubes in a single layer. Cook undisturbed for 3 to 4 minutes per side until golden-brown and crispy. Remove from the pan and set aside.Stir-fry the vegetables: Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil to the same pan. Toss in the broccoli, bell pepper, snap peas, and carrot. Stir-fry vigorously for 4 to 5 minutes until the vegetables are tender-crisp and bright green.
Push the vegetables to the outer edges of the pan. Add the minced ginger and garlic to the center. Stir constantly for 30 seconds until highly fragrant.
Pour the stir-fry sauce mixture into the pan. Bring to a quick simmer; it will thicken into a glossy glaze within 1 minute. Return the crispy tofu to the pan and toss everything gently to coat.
Garnish with green onions and sesame seeds. Serve hot over rice or noodles.
aliases: VEGETABLE AND TOFU STIRFRY WITH STICKY PLUM SAUCE (GF/DF)

title: BEET & GOAT CHEESE WITH RED WINE VINAIGRETTE

ingredients.name: fresh baby spinach or mixed greens
toasted sliced almondsRed Wine Vinaigrette: 1/3 cup extra-virgin olive oil
salt, and freshly cracked black pepper (to taste

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (204°C). Trim the stems off the beets and wash them thoroughly. Wrap each beet tightly in aluminum foil and roast on a baking sheet for 45 minutes until a fork easily pierces the center. Let cool, rub the skins off with a paper towel, and slice into bite-sized wedges.
Combine the extra-virgin olive oil, red wine vinegar, Dijon mustard, honey, salt, and black pepper in a small bowl or jar. Whisk vigorously until the mixture emulsifies.
Arrange a bed of fresh baby spinach or mixed greens on a large serving platter or split into individual salad bowls.
Scatter the cooled, sliced beet wedges evenly across the greens. Top with the crumbled goat cheese and toasted sliced almonds.
Drizzle the red wine vinaigrette over the salad components just before serving, tossing gently to coat the leaves.

title: CHAI POACHED PEARS WITH CINNAMON TOPPING

ingredients.name: firm , ripe pears (such as Bosc or Anjou), peeled with stems left intactPoaching Liquid: 3 cups water
chai tea bags (or 2 tbsp loose-leaf chai blend)
heavy cream or plain Greek yogurt (for a dollop base)

steps: Bring the water to a boil in a medium saucepan. Remove from heat, drop in the chai tea bags, and let steep for 7 minutes. Remove and squeeze the tea bags to extract all the flavor.
Stir the sugar, cinnamon stick, star anise, and whole cloves into the hot chai tea. Bring the mixture back to a gentle simmer over medium-low heat until the sugar dissolves completely.

Slice a tiny sliver off the bottom of each peeled pear so they can stand upright. Submerge the pears into the simmering chai liquid. Cover the pan and cook for 20 to 25 minutes, turning them occasionally, until they are fork-tender but not mushy.

Lift the pears out of the liquid and set them aside on a plate. Crank the stove heat to high and boil the remaining chai liquid for 8 to 10 minutes until it reduces into a glossy, pourable syrup. Remove the whole spices.
Whip the heavy cream with the maple syrup and ground cinnamon until soft peaks form (or simply stir the cinnamon and maple syrup directly into Greek yogurt for a tangy spin).

Stand an upright poached pear in a shallow dessert bowl. Drizzle a generous spoonful of the warm, reduced chai syrup over the top. Add a dollop of the cinnamon cream to the side and scatter the toasted sliced almonds over the plate.

title: SEAFOOD CHOWDER

ingredients.name: firm white fish like cod or haddock (cut into 1-inch chunks)
all-purpose flourLiquids: 2 cups clam juice (or high-quality seafood broth)
finely chopped fresh chives or parsley

steps: In a large Dutch oven or heavy-bottomed pot over medium heat, cook the diced bacon until crispy (about 5 to 6 minutes). Use a slotted spoon to remove the bacon bits and set them aside, leaving the rendered fat in the pot.Sauté the aromatics: Add the butter to the bacon fat. Once melted, toss in the diced onion and celery. Sauté for 5 minutes until the vegetables are soft and translucent.
Sprinkle the flour evenly over the vegetables. Stir continuously for 2 minutes to cook out the raw flour taste; the mixture will form a thick paste.

Slowly pour in the clam juice while whisking vigorously to prevent lumps. Stir in the whole milk, heavy cream, diced potatoes, bay leaf, and thyme sprigs. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15 minutes until the potatoes are fork-tender.

Turn the heat down to low. Stir in the fish chunks, shrimp, and scallops. Simmer gently, uncovered, for 5 to 6 minutes. Do not boil, or the seafood will turn rubbery. The chowder is ready when the shrimp are pink and the fish flakes easily.

Remove the bay leaf and thyme sprigs. Taste and season with salt and black pepper. Ladle the hot chowder into bowls, top generously with the reserved crispy bacon bits, and scatter fresh chives over the top.
aliases: SEAFOOD CHOWDER IN LIGHT TOMATO BROTH (GF/DF)

title: SPICED CHICKEN, BRIE, & AVOCADO MELT

ingredients.name: chicken breast (sliced horizontally into thin cutlets)Chicken Rub: 1 tbsp olive oil
ripe avocados (sliced)The Greens & Crunch: 2 cups fresh baby arugula
soft artisan rolls (such as ciabatta, brioche, or panini buns)Spread: 4 tbsp mayonnaise or garlic aioli

steps: Pat the chicken cutlets dry with a paper towel. Rub both sides evenly with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, smoked paprika, garlic powder, onion powder, cumin, salt, and black pepper.

Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken cutlets in a single layer. Cook for 4 to 5 minutes per side until a golden crust forms and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C). Remove from heat.
Split the artisan rolls lengthwise. Spread a layer of mayonnaise or garlic aioli across the inside surfaces of both the top and bottom buns.

Place the hot, cooked chicken cutlets directly onto the bottom buns. Layer the sliced Brie cheese immediately on top of the warm chicken so it begins to soften. Arrange the fresh avocado slices directly over the cheese.

Place the open-faced bottom halves under an oven broiler set to high for 1 to 2 minutes, or compress the assembled sandwiches in a preheated panini press until the bread turns golden-brown and the Brie is melted.
Top the melted cheese and avocado layer with a handful of fresh baby arugula. Close the sandwiches with the top buns, slice diagonally, and serve hot.
aliases: SPICED CHICKEN AND BRIE SANDWICH WITH AVOCADO MAYO

title: VEGETABLE PAELLA

ingredients.name: Bomba rice (or short-grain Calasparra / Arborio rice)
dry white wineLiquids: 3 1/2 cups low-sodium vegetable broth (warmed)

steps: Crush the saffron threads slightly and place them in a small cup. Pour 3 tablespoons of warm broth over them and let steep for 10 minutes to release its rich aroma and golden hue.Sauté the aromatics: Heat the olive oil in a traditional 12-to-14-inch paella pan (or a wide, shallow skillet) over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion, red bell pepper, and green bell pepper for 6 to 8 minutes until soft and slightly caramelized.
Stir in the minced garlic, grated tomato, and smoked paprika. Cook for 3 minutes, stirring constantly, until the mixture darkens into a thick, fragrant paste (the sofrito).
Pour in the white wine to deglaze the pan, scraping up any stuck bits. Add the Bomba rice and stir for 2 minutes, ensuring every grain is translucent and coated in the flavorful oil.

Pour in the warm vegetable broth, the bloomed saffron liquid, artichoke hearts, and green beans. Shake the pan gently to distribute the ingredients evenly. Do not stir the rice again from this point forward. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to medium-low and simmer uncovered for 15 to 18 minutes until the liquid is mostly absorbed.

Scatter the frozen peas over the top. Turn the heat up to medium-high for the last 2 minutes. Listen for a distinct crackling sound and smell a deeply toasted aroma—this forms the socarrat, the highly prized crispy rice crust at the bottom of the pan. Turn off the heat.
Cover the pan tightly with a clean kitchen towel or foil and let it rest undisturbed for 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh parsley and lemon wedges before serving directly from the pan.
aliases: VEGETABLE PAELLA.  ASSORTED GARDEN VEGETABLES WITH ORZO PASTA IN TOMATO ACCENTS (DF)

title: CITRUS GREENS WITH CHOICE DRESSING

ingredients.name: fresh spring mix or baby spinach
mandarin oranges or clementines (peeled and separated into segments)
ruby red grapefruit (peeled and sliced into rounds)
shaved red onionOption A: Zesty Citrus-Herb Vinaigrette: 1/4 cup extra-virgin olive oil
finely chopped fresh mint or parsley
salt, and black pepperOption B: Creamy Avocado-Lime

steps: Peel the citrus fruits completely, ensuring you remove as much of the bitter white pith as possible. Slice the grapefruit into thin wheels and separate the mandarins into clean segments.Whisk Option A (Vinaigrette): Combine all vinaigrette ingredients in a small jar. Shake vigorously for 30 seconds until the honey and citrus juices emulsify completely with the olive oil.Blend Option B (Creamy): Place the avocado, Greek yogurt, lime juice, cilantro, garlic, water, and salt into a small food processor or blender. Blend on high until completely smooth and velvety.
Arrange the salad greens across a wide serving platter. Scatter the sliced cucumber and shaved red onions evenly over the top.
Arrange the bright orange segments and pink grapefruit wheels over the bed of greens to create a visual centerpiece.
Keep the selected dressing on the side. Drizzle over individual portions just before eating to ensure the delicate salad leaves stay perfectly crisp.
aliases: BABY GREENS WITH ASSORTED CITRUS, RED ONION AND TOMATO.  CHOICE DRESSING (GF/DF)

title: KEY LIME MOUSSE

ingredients.name: The Citrus Base: 1/3 cup fresh Key lime juice (or standard lime juice)
Key lime zestThe Whip: 1 cup heavy whipping cream (cold)
graham crackers (crushed into crumbs), lime slices, or extra zest

steps: In a large bowl, whisk together the sweetened condensed milk, fresh Key lime juice, and lime zest. The acid from the lime juice will react with the condensed milk, causing it to thicken. Set aside.
In a separate chilled bowl, combine the heavy whipping cream, powdered sugar, and vanilla extract. Use an electric hand mixer on medium-high speed to whip the cream until stiff peaks form.

Gently spoon about one-third of the whipped cream into the lime mixture, folding it in with a rubber spatula to lighten the base. Add the remaining whipped cream and fold very gently just until combined and uniform in color. Avoid over-mixing to keep the mousse light and airy.
Spoon crushed graham cracker crumbs into the bottom of dessert glasses. Pipe or spoon the lime mousse evenly over the crumbs.
Cover the glasses loosely with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 2 hours to allow the mousse to set completely.
Garnish each glass with a light dusting of extra graham cracker crumbs, a pinch of lime zest, and a thin lime wheel just before serving.

title: THAI COCONUT & VEGETABLE

ingredients.name: stalks lemongrass (bruised and cut into 2-inch pieces), 1-inch piece galangal or fresh ginger (sliced into coins)

steps: Sauté the curry paste: Heat the vegetable oil in a large pot over medium heat. Add the red curry paste and minced garlic, stirring constantly for 1 minute until highly fragrant.

Pour in the vegetable broth and coconut milk. Add the lemongrass pieces, galangal slices, and torn kaffir lime leaves. Bring to a gentle boil, then reduce heat to low and simmer uncovered for 5 to 7 minutes to fully infuse the broth.

Toss the shiitake mushrooms, red bell pepper, and broccoli florets into the pot. Simmer for 3 minutes. Add the baby bok choy and cook for an additional 2 minutes until all vegetables are tender-crisp.
Stir in the soy sauce and coconut sugar. Turn off the heat and stir in the fresh lime juice to bring a bright contrast to the rich coconut base.
Remove the woody lemongrass pieces, galangal coins, and kaffir lime leaves from the pot. Ladle the hot soup into deep bowls and garnish generously with fresh cilantro and optional sliced chilis.

title: LEMONGRASS CHICKEN

ingredients.name: boneless, skinless chicken thighs (cut into bite-sized pieces)Lemongrass Marinade: 2 stalks fresh lemongrass (finely minced, white parts only)
chili flakes (optional)Cooking: 1 tbsp vegetable oil (for the pan)
fresh cilantro, lemon or lime wedges

steps: Cut off the tough top leaves and the woody base of the lemongrass stalks. Peel away the dry outer layers until you reach the pale, soft inner core. Finely mince or grate this core to release its aromatic oils.

In a large bowl, whisk together the minced lemongrass, garlic, shallot, fish sauce, soy sauce, brown sugar, 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil, and optional chili flakes. Toss the chicken pieces in the marinade until evenly coated. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes (or up to 4 hours).
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.
Add the chicken to the hot pan in a single layer, discarding excess marinade. Let it cook undisturbed for 4 to 5 minutes to develop a deeply browned, caramelized crust.
Flip the chicken pieces and stir-fry for another 5 to 6 minutes until the chicken is cooked through and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).
Transfer the chicken to a serving platter. Garnish with sliced green onions, fresh cilantro, and a squeeze of fresh lemon or lime juice.

title: CHICKPEA & VEGETABLE CURRY
steps: Sauté the aromatics: Heat the coconut oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Sauté the diced onion for 5 minutes until soft and translucent. Stir in the minced garlic and grated ginger, cooking for 1 additional minute until highly fragrant.
Add the curry powder, cumin, turmeric, and salt directly to the onion mixture. Stir constantly for 30 seconds to toast the spices, which unlocks their full depth of flavor.

Pour in the canned diced tomatoes (with their juices) and the diced sweet potato cubes. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to medium-low, cover the pot, and simmer for 10 minutes until the sweet potatoes begin to soften.

Stir the rinsed chickpeas and chopped red bell pepper into the pot. Pour the full-fat coconut milk over the mixture and stir well to combine. Simmer uncovered for 5 to 7 minutes until the sauce thickens slightly and the sweet potatoes are completely fork-tender.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the fresh baby spinach and cover the pot for 2 minutes, allowing the residual heat to gently wilt the leaves.
Stir in the fresh lime juice to cut through the rich coconut base. Garnish with chopped fresh cilantro and serve hot.

title: GREEN MANGO SALAD WITH POPPYSEED DRESSING

ingredients.name: large green mangoes (unripe and firm to the touch, peeled and julienned)
finely chopped fresh mint or cilantroPoppyseed
neutral oil (like avocado or canola oil)

steps: Combine the neutral oil, apple cider vinegar, honey, Dijon mustard, and salt in a small bowl. Whisk vigorously until the mixture emulsifies. Stir in the poppy seeds and set aside.
Peel the green mangoes. Use a mandoline, julienne peeler, or a sharp knife to slice the firm flesh into thin, matchstick-sized strips, stopping when you reach the hard inner pit.
In a large mixing bowl, toss together the julienned green mangoes, thinly sliced red bell pepper, and red onion.
Pour the poppyseed dressing over the salad components. Toss thoroughly to ensure the mango strips absorb the dressing.
Fold in the fresh chopped mint or cilantro just before serving. Let the salad sit for 5 minutes at room temperature to soften the mango slightly.
aliases: MIXED GARDEN GREENS WITH FRESH ASSORTED VEGETABLES.  CHOICE DRESSING (GF/DF)

title: PINEAPPLE CAKE WITH CITRUS TOPPING

ingredients.name: 20 oz pineapple slices, drained with juice reserved

steps: Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Pour the melted butter directly into the bottom of a 9-inch round cake pan. Sprinkle the brown sugar evenly over the butter.
Lay the drained pineapple slices in a single layer on top of the brown sugar layer. Place one slice in the center and arrange the others around it. Set aside.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt.
In a large bowl, cream the softened butter and granulated sugar together with an electric mixer until light and fluffy. Beat in the eggs one at a time, followed by the vanilla extract.
Alternate adding the dry flour mixture, milk, and reserved pineapple juice to the wet ingredients, mixing on low speed just until a smooth batter forms.
Spoon the batter carefully over the arranged pineapple slices, smoothing the top with a spatula. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean.

Let the cake cool in the pan for exactly 10 minutes. Run a knife around the edges, place a large serving plate upside down over the pan, and quickly invert it. Let the pan sit for 1 minute so the caramel glaze drizzles down before lifting the pan.
Whisk the powdered sugar, lime juice, and orange zest together in a small cup. Drizzle this bright citrus glaze over the warm pineapple cake before slicing.

title: FRENCH LENTIL

ingredients.name: fresh lemon juice or red wine vinegar

steps: Sauté the mirepoix: Heat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the diced onion, carrots, and celery. Cook for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are soft and the onions are translucent.
Stir in the minced garlic and tomato paste. Cook for 2 minutes, stirring constantly, until the tomato paste darkens into a deep brick-red color and smells fragrant.
If using wine, pour it in now to deglaze the pan, using a wooden spoon to scrape up any browned bits stuck to the bottom. Simmer for 2 minutes until most of the alcohol evaporates.

Add the rinsed French green lentils, broth, bay leaf, and thyme sprigs to the pot. Bring the mixture to a rolling boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover tightly and simmer for 30 to 35 minutes until the lentils are tender but still hold their shape.Thicken (Optional): Remove the bay leaf and thyme sprigs. For a thicker texture, transfer 1 cup of the soup to a blender, puree until smooth, and stir it back into the pot.
Turn off the heat. Stir in the fresh lemon juice or red wine vinegar and chopped parsley. Season with additional salt and pepper to taste. Ladle into bowls and serve hot.

title: CAPRESE STYLE CHICKEN PLATE

ingredients.name: small chicken breasts (boneless, skinless, pounded to an even 1/2-inch thickness)Chicken Seasoning: 1 tbsp olive oil
thick slices fresh mozzarella cheese (from a log)
high-quality balsamic glaze (store-bought reduction or homemade

steps: Pat the chicken breasts dry with paper towels. Rub both sides evenly with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, dried Italian seasoning, garlic powder, salt, and black pepper.
Heat a large, oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add the chicken breasts in a single layer. Cook undisturbed for 5 to 6 minutes until a deeply golden crust forms on the bottom.
Flip the chicken breasts over. Immediately top each breast with a slice of fresh tomato, followed by a slice of fresh mozzarella cheese.

Cover the skillet tightly with a lid and reduce the heat to medium-low. Let cook for 4 to 5 minutes until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 165°F (74°C) and the mozzarella is completely melted and gooey.
Transfer the chicken plates to a clean serving platter and let them rest for 3 minutes to lock in the juices.
Just before serving, tuck fresh basil leaves under and around the melted cheese layer on each breast. Drizzle the glossy balsamic glaze generously across the top of the entire plate.

title: COD WITH LEMON&HERB SAUCE (GF/DF)
steps: Pat the cod fillets thoroughly dry with paper towels. Season both sides evenly with onion powder, salt, and white pepper.

Heat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Add the cod fillets and sear undisturbed for 3 to 4 minutes until a golden crust forms and the fish naturally releases from the pan.
Carefully flip the fillets using a fish spatula. Cook for another 2 to 3 minutes until opaque throughout and flaky. Transfer to a warm platter and cover loosely with foil.
Turn the skillet down to medium heat. Add the 2 tablespoons of extra-virgin olive oil. Sauté the minced shallots and garlic for 2 minutes until soft and fragrant.
Pour in the vegetable broth, lemon juice, and lemon zest. Bring to a simmer for 2 minutes. Whisk in the cornstarch slurry and let the sauce bubble for 1 minute until it thickens into a glossy glaze.
Turn off the heat. Stir the fresh parsley and dill directly into the warm sauce.
Pour the velvety lemon-herb sauce generously over the seared cod fillets and serve hot.
aliases: BAKED COD LOIN WITH LEMON AND HERB SAUCE (GF/DF)

title: MEDITERRANEAN CHICKPEA

ingredients.name: (15 oz each) chickpeas / garbanzo beans (rinsed and drained)The
orange or red bell pepper (diced)The Brine & Cream: 1/2 cup Kalamata olives (pitted and halved)

steps: Pour the canned chickpeas into a colander. Rinse thoroughly under cold water and let them drain completely. Pat them dry with a paper towel so the dressing sticks to them.
In a small bowl or jar, combine the extra-virgin olive oil, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, dried oregano, salt, and black pepper. Whisk vigorously until the dressing emulsifies.
In a large mixing bowl, combine the drained chickpeas, diced cucumber, halved cherry tomatoes, diced red onion, and diced bell pepper.
Toss the halved Kalamata olives and chopped fresh parsley into the bowl. Gently fold in the crumbled feta cheese.
Pour the lemon-oregano dressing over the salad. Toss gently with large spoons until every ingredient is evenly coated.
Let the salad sit at room temperature for 10 to 15 minutes before serving. This allows the chickpeas to absorb the zesty dressing and fields a much deeper flavor.
aliases: MEDITERRANEAN SPICED CHICKPEA WITH BABY SPINACH, PEPPERS AND ONIONS IN A LEMON POPPYSEED DRESSING (GF/DF)

title: APPLE TART

ingredients.name: pre-made puff pastry (thawed) or 1 unbaked 9-inch pie crust

steps: Preheat oven to 400°F (204°C). If using puff pastry, roll it out slightly on a sheet of parchment paper into a 10-inch square, then place it on a baking sheet. If using standard pie pastry, press it firmly into a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
Toss the thin apple slices in a large bowl with the melted butter, granulated sugar, ground cinnamon, and vanilla extract until every slice is evenly coated.

Starting from the outer edge of the pastry, lay the apple slices down in a tightly overlapping circular pattern, working toward the center. Shingle them tightly; they will shrink slightly as they bake.
Place the tart in the oven and bake for 30 to 35 minutes. It is ready when the pastry edges are a deep golden-brown and the apple slices are tender and slightly caramelized on the tips.
While the tart is hot out of the oven, stir the apricot preserves and hot water together in a small cup until smooth. Use a pastry brush to gently dab the glossy glaze over the warm apples.
Let the tart cool in the pan for 15 minutes before removing the outer ring. Slice and serve warm.

title: GRILLED CHICKEN SOUVLAKI WITH ROASTED SPUDS AND GARLIC SAUCE (GF)

ingredients.name: Chicken breasts or thighs ((700g), boneless, skinless, cut into 1-inch chunks)
Sea salt and black pepper (salt, 1/2 tsp pepper)
Wooden or metal skewers (–8 piecesFor the Roasted Spuds (Potatoes):)
Russet or Yukon Gold potatoes ((900g), peeled and cut into thick wedges)
Chicken or vegetable stock (/2 cup (certified gluten-free))
Garlic cloves (/2 cup, peeled (germs removed if green))
Neutral oil ((like avocado, canola, or safflower oil))

steps: 1. Marinate the ChickenWhisk 1/4 cup olive oil, 3 tbsp lemon juice, minced garlic, 2 tbsp oregano, salt, and pepper in a bowl.Toss in the chicken chunks until coated.Cover and refrigerate for 1 to 2 hours.Note: If using wooden skewers, soak them in water now.

2. Roast the SpudsPreheat your oven to 400°F (205°C).Place the potato wedges into a large baking dish in a single layer.Whisk together the 1/3 cup olive oil, stock, 1/4 cup lemon juice, 1 tbsp oregano, garlic powder, and salt.Pour the liquid mixture directly over the potatoes.Roast for 45 to 50 minutes, turning once halfway through, until the liquid is absorbed and the edges are crispy and golden-brown.

3. Whip the Garlic SaucePlace the garlic cloves and 1 tsp salt into a food processor.Pulse until finely minced, scraping down the sides.With the motor running on low, slowly drizzle 1/2 cup of the neutral oil in a paper-thin, continuous stream until it thickens.Alternate adding a few drops of lemon juice and more oil, pouring very slowly so the sauce emulsifies into a fluffy, white paste (like mayonnaise).If it gets too thick or warm, add 1 tablespoon of ice water to smooth it out. Set aside in the fridge.

4. Grill the ChickenThread the marinated chicken tightly onto your skewers.Heat an outdoor grill or indoor grill pan to medium-high.Grill the skewers for 8 to 10 minutes, turning every few minutes, until charred and the internal temperature hits 165°F (74°C).
5. Assemble the PlatterServe the hot, smoky chicken skewers alongside a pile of the crispy lemon spuds.Add a generous dollop of the chilled garlic sauce on the side for dipping.

title: VEGETABLE PAD THAI WITH TOFU

ingredients.name: Palm sugar ((substitute brown sugar if needed))
Fish sauce ((substitute soy sauce for a strict vegetarian version)For the Stir-Fry:)
Extra-firm tofu ((225g), drained, patted dry, and cut into bite-sized cubes)
Vegetables (broccoli florets, 1 medium carrot (julienned), and 1 red bell pepper (sliced thin))
Eggs (lightly beaten (skip for a vegan version))
Garlic chives or scallions (cut into 1-inch pieces)
High-smoke point oil ((like peanut, canola, or vegetable oil)For the Garnish:)

steps: 1. Prep the Rice NoodlesPlace the dry rice noodles in a large bowl and cover them completely with lukewarm water.Let them soak for 30 to 45 minutes while you prepare the other ingredients. They should be pliable but still firm to the touch. Drain and set aside. Do not boil them, or they will turn mushy in the wok.

2. Mix the SauceCombine the tamarind paste, palm sugar, and fish sauce (or soy sauce) in a small saucepan over low heat.Stir gently until the sugar completely dissolves and the mixture thickens slightly. Remove from heat.

3. Sear the TofuHeat 1 tablespoon of oil in a large wok or deep skillet over medium-high heat.Add the tofu cubes and cook for about 5 minutes, turning occasionally, until they are golden-brown and crispy on all sides. Transfer the tofu to a plate.

4. Stir-Fry the Aromatics and VeggiesAdd the remaining 2 tablespoons of oil to the same wok over high heat.Toss in the minced garlic and sliced shallots, stirring rapidly for 30 seconds until fragrant.Add the broccoli, carrots, and bell peppers. Stir-fry for 2 to 3 minutes until they are bright and crisp-tender.

5. Toss the Noodles and SaucePush the vegetables to one side of the wok.Add the drained noodles and the prepared sauce directly into the empty space.Use tongs to toss the noodles continuously for 2 minutes, drawing in the sauce and vegetables, until the noodles absorb the liquid and become tender.

6. Scramble the EggsPush everything to the outer rim of the wok to create an opening in the center.Pour the beaten eggs into the clearing. Let them sit undisturbed for 30 seconds, then gently scramble them until soft curds form.Fold the eggs into the noodle mixture.

7. Final Toss and ServeReturn the crispy tofu to the wok, along with the bean sprouts and garlic chives.Toss everything together for 1 final minute until the sprouts just begin to wilt.Plate immediately and garnish generously with crushed peanuts, a sprinkle of chili flakes, and a fresh squeeze of lime juice.

title: CUCUMBER & DILL WITH LEMON YOGURT DRESSING

ingredients.name: Red onion (/4 medium, sliced into paper-thin half-moons)
Plain Greek yogurt (/2 cup (whole milk or 2% works best))
Fresh lemon juice ((about half a juicy lemon))
Fresh dill (finely chopped (plus extra for garnish))
Honey or maple syrup (/2 tsp (optional, to balance the acidity))
Sea salt (/2 tsp (plus more for sweating the cucumbers))

steps: 1. Sweat the Cucumbers (Optional but Recommended)Place the thinly sliced cucumbers in a colander in the sink.Sprinkle them with 1/2 teaspoon of salt and toss gently to coat.Let sit for 10 minutes to draw out excess water, preventing a watery salad.Pat the cucumbers dry with a clean kitchen towel or paper towels.

2. Whisk the DressingIn a large mixing bowl, combine the Greek yogurt, lemon juice, lemon zest, minced garlic, chopped fresh dill, and optional honey.Whisk until the dressing is completely smooth.Season with the 1/2 teaspoon of salt and black pepper.

3. Toss and CombineAdd the dried cucumber slices and thin red onion slices directly into the bowl with the yogurt dressing.Toss gently with a spatula or large spoon until every slice is evenly coated.
4. Chill and ServeServe immediately, or cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes to let the flavors deepen.Garnish with an extra sprinkle of fresh dill and a crack of black pepper right before serving.

title: STRAWBERRIES AND CREAM PARFAIT

ingredients.name: Almond flour or finely chopped almonds (/4 cup)
Fresh strawberries ((450g), hull removed and finely diced)
Fresh lemon juice ((brightens the fruit flavor)For the Vanilla Cream Layer:)
Mascarpone cheese or Greek yogurt (/4 cup (adds body and stability to the layers))

steps: 1. Bake the CrumblePreheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).In a small bowl, mix the oats, almond flour, melted butter, maple syrup, and salt until clumpy.Spread the mixture onto a parchment-lined baking sheet.Bake for 8 to 10 minutes until golden-brown and fragrant. Let it cool completely to become crispy.

2. Macerate the BerriesToss the finely diced strawberries with the sugar and lemon juice in a medium bowl.Let them sit at room temperature for 10 minutes until they release their bright red, glossy juices.

3. Whip the Vanilla CreamAdd the chilled heavy cream, mascarpone (or Greek yogurt), powdered sugar, and vanilla extract into a cold mixing bowl.Beat with an electric hand mixer on medium-high speed until it forms medium-to-stiff peaks. The mascarpone keeps the cream thick so the parfait layers stay cleanly separated.

4. Assemble the ParfaitsTake 4 small dessert glasses or mason jars.Spoon 1 tablespoon of the cooled crispy crumble into the bottom of each glass.Add a generous layer of the macerated strawberries, ensuring you spoon in some of the sweet syrup.Pipe or spoon a thick layer of the vanilla cream over the berries.Repeat the layers (crumble, strawberries, cream) one more time until the glasses are full.

5. Garnish and ChillTop each parfait with a final sprinkle of the remaining crumble and a whole strawberry or a mint leaf.Serve immediately for maximum crunch, or chill in the refrigerator for up to 2 hours.

title: WONTON SOUP (DF)

ingredients.name: Fresh ginger (-inch knob, sliced into thick coins and smashed)
Baby bok choy (halved lengthwise (or 2 cups chopped spinach))

steps: 1. Simmer the BrothCombine the stock, smashed ginger slices, and smashed garlic in a large pot.Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 10 minutes to infuse the aromatic flavors.Stir in the 1 tablespoon of soy sauce, then use a slotted spoon to remove and discard the ginger and garlic pieces.

2. Cook the Pre-made WontonsBring a separate pot of plain water to a rolling boil. Note: Boiling the wontons in water first keeps your soup broth beautifully clear instead of cloudy.Carefully drop your pre-made wontons into the boiling water.If fresh: Cook for 3 to 4 minutes.If frozen: Cook for 5 to 6 minutes (no need to thaw them first).They are done when they float to the top and the wrappers look wrinkled and translucent.

3. Blanch the GreensDuring the last 1 minute of the wontons boiling, drop the halved baby bok choy or spinach into the same boiling water to blanch it until bright green.Use a slotted spoon to lift the cooked wontons and greens out of the water and place them directly into your serving bowls.
4. Assemble and ServeLadle the hot, seasoned ginger broth directly over the wontons and greens in each bowl.Finish each bowl with a few drops of toasted sesame oil and a handful of fresh scallions.

title: HOISIN SHAVED BEEF WITH BROCCOLI OVER RICE (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: Gluten-free hoisin sauce (/3 cup (ensure the label reads certified)
Shaved beef (ribeye or flank) ((450g), sliced paper-thin)

steps: 1. Prep the Shaved BeefToss the paper-thin shaved beef with the 1 teaspoon of cornstarch or arrowroot powder in a medium bowl until lightly coated. Chef's Tip: If slicing the meat yourself, freeze the beef block for 30 minutes beforehand to make shaving it thin effortless.
2. Whisk the SauceIn a small bowl, whisk together the gluten-free hoisin, tamari, toasted sesame oil, rice vinegar, honey, and water until completely smooth. Set aside.

3. Steam-Fry the BroccoliHeat 1 tablespoon of the neutral oil (avocado or canola) in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat.Add the broccoli florets and stir-fry for 1 minute.Pour in 2 tablespoons of extra water, immediately cover the pan with a lid, and let the broccoli steam for 2 minutes until it turns bright green and crisp-tender.Remove the broccoli from the pan and set it aside on a plate.

4. Sear the BeefTurn the pan heat up to high and add the remaining 1 tablespoon of neutral oil.Spread the shaved beef out in a single layer across the hot pan.Let it sear undisturbed for 1 minute to get a nice brown crust, then toss and stir-fry for another 1 minute until it is just barely pink.Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the center of the pan, stirring rapidly for 30 seconds until highly fragrant.

5. Toss and ThickenReturn the cooked broccoli to the pan with the beef.Pour the prepared hoisin sauce mixture directly over the ingredients.Toss everything continuously for 1 to 2 minutes. The sauce will bubble, combine with the starch on the beef, and quickly thicken into a glossy glaze that coats every piece. Remove from the heat.

6. Assemble and ServeDivide the hot cooked rice into individual serving bowls.Spoon a generous portion of the hoisin beef and broccoli over the rice, scraping all the extra glossy sauce from the pan.Garnish with the toasted sesame seeds and sliced scallions.

title: STUFFED BOLOGNESE ZUCCHINI BOATS

ingredients.name: Carrot (finely grated or minced (traditional bolognese element))

steps: 1. Prep the Oven and ZucchiniPreheat your oven to 400°F (205°C).Use a small spoon to gently scoop out the flesh from the center of each halved zucchini, creating a "boat" with a 1/4-inch thick shell.Finely chop the scooped-out zucchini flesh and set it aside to use in the sauce.Place the empty zucchini boats cut-side up in a large 9x13-inch baking dish.

2. Brown the Meat and AromaticsHeat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.Add the ground beef (or sausage) and diced onion. Cook for 5 to 6 minutes, breaking the meat apart with a spoon, until the meat is browned and the onions are soft. Drain any excess grease.

3. Simmer the Bolognese SauceStir in the minced garlic, grated carrot, and the reserved chopped zucchini flesh. Cook for 2 minutes.Add the marinara sauce, tomato paste, Italian seasoning, salt, and black pepper.Reduce heat to low and let the sauce simmer for 10 minutes until thick and flavorful.
4. Stuff the BoatsSpoon the warm bolognese sauce generously into each zucchini boat, pressing down slightly so it fills the cavities completely.

5. Bake and MeltCover the baking dish tightly with aluminum foil and bake for 20 minutes until the zucchini shells are tender when pierced with a fork.Remove the foil, sprinkle the shredded mozzarella and grated Parmesan evenly over the top of each boat.Return to the oven uncovered for 5 minutes (or broil on high for 2 minutes) until the cheese is melted, bubbly, and golden-brown.
6. Garnish and ServeLet the boats cool for 5 minutes so they handle easily.Garnish with fresh chopped parsley before serving.

title: NAPPA CABBAGE SALAD WITH ASIAN VINAIGRETTE

ingredients.name: Napa cabbage (head (about 4–5 cups), thinly sliced or shredded)
Neutral oil ((like avocado, canola, or vegetable oil))

steps: 1. Shred the CabbageCut the Napa cabbage in half lengthwise.Remove the tough core at the bottom.Slice it crosswise into thin ribbons.Place the shredded cabbage into a large salad bowl.
2. Prep the Mix-InsAdd the julienned carrots, sliced scallions, and chopped cilantro directly to the bowl with the cabbage.Toss the vegetables together so they are evenly distributed.

3. Whisk the VinaigretteCombine the rice vinegar, soy sauce, toasted sesame oil, neutral oil, honey, grated ginger, and minced garlic in a small bowl or a mason jar.Whisk vigorously or secure the jar lid and shake well until the dressing emulsifies and the honey is completely dissolved.

4. Dress and GarnishDrizzle the vinaigrette over the salad.Toss well to ensure all the cabbage ribbons are coated.Top with the toasted sliced almonds and sesame seeds right before serving to keep them completely crunchy.

title: COCONUT CREAM PIE

ingredients.name: Shredded sweetened coconut ((plus 1/2 cup extra for toasting))
Coconut extract (/2 tsp (optional, for deeper flavor))

steps: 1. Toast the CoconutPreheat your oven to 350°F (175°C).Spread the 1/2 cup of extra shredded coconut onto a baking sheet.Bake for 5 to 7 minutes, tossing occasionally, until golden-brown.Remove from the oven and let cool completely. Set aside for the garnish.
2. Whisk the Custard BaseWhisk the granulated sugar, cornstarch, half-and-half, coconut milk, and egg yolks together in a large saucepan until completely smooth.

3. Thicken the CustardPlace the saucepan over medium heat.Whisk constantly for 6 to 8 minutes as the mixture heats up.Bring it to a gentle boil. Once bubbling, cook for exactly 1 more minute while whisking vigorously. The custard will become very thick and glossy. Remove from heat.
4. Add the FlavoringsImmediately stir in the unsalted butter, vanilla extract, optional coconut extract, and the 1 cup of untoasted shredded coconut until the butter melts completely.

5. Chill the PiePour the warm custard directly into your pre-baked, cooled pie crust.Smooth the top with a spatula.Press a piece of plastic wrap directly onto the surface of the custard to prevent a skin from forming.Refrigerate for at least 4 hours (or overnight) until cold and firmly set.

6. Top and ServeAdd the cold heavy whipping cream and powdered sugar into a chilled mixing bowl.Beat on medium-high speed until stiff peaks form.Remove the plastic wrap from the pie.Spread or pipe the whipped cream generously over the chilled coconut custard layer.Sprinkle the cooled, toasted coconut over the whipped cream before slicing.

title: BLACK BEAN SOUP WITH SCALLION CRÈME FRAICHE

ingredients.name: Canned black beans ((15 oz / 425g each), rinsed and drained)
Black pepper (/2 tspCrème fraîche: 1/2 cup (substitute sour cream if needed))
Scallions (green onions) (green parts only, finely minced)

steps: 1. Sauté the AromaticsHeat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.Add the diced onion and red bell pepper. Cook for 5 to 6 minutes until the vegetables are soft and translucent.Stir in the minced garlic, ground cumin, chili powder, smoked paprika, and dried oregano. Cook for 1 minute until highly fragrant.

2. Simmer the SoupAdd the rinsed black beans, stock, sea salt, and black pepper to the pot. Stir well to combine.Bring the liquid to a boil, then reduce the heat to low.Cover and simmer gently for 15 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.

3. Blend the BaseRemove the pot from the heat.Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until smooth and creamy. Note: If you prefer a chunky texture, only blend half of the soup, or mash some of the beans with a wooden spoon.Stir in 1 tablespoon of the fresh lime juice. Taste and add more salt if needed.
4. Mix the Scallion Crème FraîcheWhisk the crème fraîche, finely minced scallion greens, lime zest, and 1 teaspoon of lime juice together in a small bowl until completely smooth.
5. Garnish and ServeLadle the hot black bean soup into individual bowls.Top each portion with a generous dollop (or a beautiful swirl) of the chilled scallion crème fraîche.

title: GRILLED CHICKEN BREAST WITH PICO DE GALLO (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: White onion (/2 small, finely dicedJalapeño: 1, seeded and minced (optional for heat))

steps: 1. Marinate the ChickenIn a large bowl or zip-top bag, combine 2 tablespoons of olive oil, 2 tablespoons of lime juice, minced garlic, dried oregano, cumin, 1 teaspoon of salt, and black pepper.Add the chicken breasts and toss until thoroughly coated.Let the chicken marinate at room temperature for 15 to 20 minutes (or up to 2 hours in the refrigerator).

2. Prepare the Fresh Pico de GalloIn a medium bowl, combine the diced tomatoes, white onion, minced jalapeño, and chopped cilantro.Stir in the remaining 2 tablespoons of lime juice and 1/2 teaspoon of sea salt.Toss well and set aside. Note: Letting the salsa sit for 10–15 minutes allows the salt to draw out the tomato juices for a better flavor.

3. Grill the ChickenPreheat your outdoor grill or an indoor cast-iron grill pan to medium-high heat. Lightly brush the grates with the remaining 1 tablespoon of oil.Place the chicken breasts on the grill. Cook for 6 to 8 minutes per side.The chicken is done when it is opaque throughout and the internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).

4. Rest and ServeTransfer the grilled chicken to a plate and let it rest for 5 minutes. This allows the juices to redistribute, ensuring the meat stays tender.Serve the chicken breasts whole or sliced, topped generously with the fresh Pico de Gallo.
aliases: GRILLED CHICKEN BREAST WITH CITRUS SALSA

title: PASTA PRIMAVERA.  PENNE PASTA TOSSED WITH GARDEN VEGETABLES IN OLIVE GARLIC SAUCE (DF)

ingredients.name: Extra-virgin olive oil (/3 cup (high quality is best for the sauce))
Red bell pepper (sliced into thin strips)
Yellow squash or zucchini (quartered and sliced)
Sea salt ((plus more for the pasta water))
Red pepper flakes (/4 tsp (optional, for a tiny hint of heat))
Fresh basil (/4 cup, torn or chiffonade for garnish🥣)

steps: 1. Boil the PastaBring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil.Add the penne pasta and cook according to the package instructions until it is al dente (usually 9 to 11 minutes).Crucial Step: Reserve 1/2 cup of the starchy pasta water right before draining. Drain the pasta and set it aside.

2. Sauté the Harder VeggiesWhile the pasta cooks, heat 2 tablespoons of the olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.Add the sliced carrots and broccoli florets. Sauté for 3 minutes until they begin to soften.

3. Add the Softer VeggiesToss the red bell pepper and yellow squash into the skillet.Cook for another 3 to 4 minutes, stirring frequently, until all the vegetables are crisp-tender and brightly colored. Transfer the vegetables to a plate.

4. Infuse the Garlic Oil SauceTurn the skillet heat down to medium-low and add the remaining olive oil.Add the thinly sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook gently for 1 to 2 minutes until the garlic is fragrant and lightly golden around the edges. Watch closely so it does not burn and turn bitter.

5. Toss Everything TogetherIncrease the heat to medium. Add the halved cherry tomatoes, Italian seasoning, sea salt, and black pepper to the garlic oil. Cook for 1 minute until the tomatoes just begin to soften.Return the sautéed vegetables and the drained penne pasta to the skillet.Pour in 1/4 cup of the reserved pasta water. Toss everything continuously for 1 to 2 minutes until the oil and water emulsify into a glossy sauce that coats the pasta. Add more pasta water if it looks too dry.
6. Garnish and ServeRemove from the heat.Toss in the fresh torn basil leaves and serve hot.

title: TOMATO AND LIME SALAD WITH CITRUS VINAIGRETTE

ingredients.name: IngredientsRoma or vine-ripened tomatoes (sliced into thick wedges or rounds)
Limes ((1 sliced paper-thin into half-wheels, 1 zested and juiced for dressing))
Red onion (/4 medium, sliced into paper-thin slivers)
Orange juice ((adds a hint of natural sweetness))
Honey or agave syrup ((balances the sharp citrus acidity))

steps: 1. Mellow the OnionsPlace the thinly sliced red onions into a small bowl of ice water for 5 minutes. This removes their harsh, raw bite while keeping them perfectly crisp. Drain and pat dry.

2. Whisk the Citrus VinaigretteIn a small bowl or jar, combine the fresh lime zest, 2 tablespoons of fresh lime juice, the orange juice, olive oil, honey, sea salt, and black pepper.Whisk vigorously until the oil and citrus juices emulsify into a smooth dressing.
3. Assemble the SaladArrange the tomato wedges and paper-thin lime slices on a large serving platter or in a shallow bowl.Scatter the crisp red onions and chopped cilantro evenly over the top.
4. Dress and ServeDrizzle the citrus vinaigrette generously over the salad just before serving.Toss gently to combine so the tomatoes can absorb the dressing without breaking apart.

title: MANGO SORBET
steps: Store-bought

title: MUSHROOM VELOUTE.  CREAMY BASE WITH SAUTEED MUSHROOMS (GF)

ingredients.name: Gluten-free 1-to-1 baking flour ((or sweet rice flour))

steps: 1. Sear the Mushroom GarnishHeat 1 tablespoon of butter and the olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat.Add 1 cup of the prettiest sliced mushrooms to the pot.Sauté undisturbed for 2 to 3 minutes until deeply browned on one side, then toss and cook for 2 more minutes.Transfer these golden mushrooms to a small plate and set aside to use as your garnish.

2. Cook the Aromatics and Base MushroomsTurn the heat down to medium and add the remaining 3 tablespoons of butter to the same pot.Add the chopped shallots and the remaining sliced mushrooms. Cook for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the mushrooms release their moisture, shrink, and turn dark brown.Stir in the minced garlic and fresh thyme leaves. Cook for 1 minute until highly fragrant.

3. Create the Gluten-Free RouxSprinkle the 3 tablespoons of gluten-free flour directly over the mushroom and onion mixture.Stir continuously for 1 to 2 minutes to coat the vegetables and cook out the raw flour taste. The mixture will look thick and pasty.

4. Simmer the VeloutéSlowly pour in the chicken or vegetable stock, whisking constantly to break up any flour lumps.Add the sea salt and black pepper.Bring the liquid to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover and simmer for 10 minutes until the soup thickens slightly.

5. Blend and Finish with CreamRemove the pot from the heat.Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until it is completely smooth, creamy, and uniform.Stir in the heavy cream. Return the pot to low heat for 1 to 2 minutes just to warm the cream through. Do not let it boil. Taste and adjust the salt if needed.
6. Garnish and ServeLadle the hot velouté into warm soup bowls.Top each portion with a spoonful of the reserved sautéed mushrooms and a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley.

title: ROAST PORK TENDERLOIN WITH APPLE FENNEL SLAW

ingredients.name: Granny Smith apple (cored and julienned (thin matchsticks))
Fresh parsley or fennel fronds (/4 cup, chopped🥣)

steps: 1. Prep and Season the PorkPreheat your oven to 400°F (205°C).Pat the pork tenderloin completely dry with paper towels.In a small bowl, mix 1 tablespoon of olive oil, the Dijon mustard, garlic powder, dried thyme, 1 teaspoon of sea salt, and black pepper into a paste.Rub this seasoning paste evenly over all sides of the pork tenderloin.

2. Sear the MeatHeat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a large, oven-safe skillet (like cast iron) over medium-high heat.Add the pork tenderloin to the hot skillet.Sear for 2 minutes per side (about 6 to 8 minutes total) until a golden-brown crust forms on all sides.

3. Roast in the OvenTransfer the entire skillet directly into the preheated oven.Roast for 12 to 15 minutes.The pork is perfectly cooked when a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part reads 145°F (63°C) for a juicy, blushing pink center.Transfer the pork to a cutting board, tent loosely with foil, and let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing.

4. Toss the Apple Fennel SlawWhile the pork roasts and rests, make the slaw.In a large bowl, whisk together the remaining 1 tablespoon of olive oil, fresh lemon juice, honey, and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon of sea salt.Add the paper-thinly sliced fennel bulb, julienned Granny Smith apple, and sliced red onion to the bowl.Toss thoroughly to coat the ingredients in the dressing so the lemon juice prevents the apples from turning brown.Fold in the chopped parsley or reserved feathery fennel fronds.

5. Slice and ServeSlice the rested pork tenderloin into 1/2-inch thick medallions.Arrange the warm pork slices on a platter and serve immediately with a generous mound of the crisp, cold apple fennel slaw right alongside it.

title: SWEET POTATO & BLACK BEAN BOWL

ingredients.name: Sweet potatoes (peeled and quartered lengthwise (approx. 28 oz))
Black beans ((15 oz), drained and rinsed)
Avocado ((from 1 small pitted, peeled, and diced))

steps: 1. Par-Boil and Season the Sweet PotatoesPlace the quartered sweet potatoes in a large pot of salted water and bring to a boil. Cook for about 8 to 10 minutes until slightly tender but still firm. Drain and let cool slightly, then cut into bite-sized cubes.In a large bowl, toss the cubed sweet potatoes with 1 teaspoon of olive oil, chili powder, chipotle powder, cumin, garlic powder, ground coriander, and a pinch of salt until evenly coated.

2. Roast the Sweet PotatoesPreheat your oven to 400°F (205°C).Spread the seasoned sweet potatoes in a single layer on a baking sheet.Roast for 15 to 20 minutes, tossing halfway through, until the edges are crispy and golden-brown.

3. Massage the KalePlace the chopped kale in a medium bowl.Drizzle with the remaining 1 teaspoon of olive oil and a pinch of salt.Use your hands to firmly massage the kale leaves for 1 to 2 minutes until they turn dark green, tender, and shrink in volume.

4. Blend the Creamy Chipotle SauceIn a blender or small food processor, combine the Greek yogurt (or dairy-free alternative), the single chipotle pepper in adobo, 2 cloves of garlic, and 2 tablespoons of lime juice.Blend on high speed until completely smooth and smoky pink.

5. Assemble the Bowls (Yield: 4 Servings)Set out 4 serving bowls. Base each bowl with 1/2 cup of hot cooked brown rice and 1/2 cup of massaged kale.Divide the roasted sweet potatoes and the rinsed black beans equally among the bowls.Top each bowl with diced avocado, quick-pickled red onions, and fresh cilantro leaves.Drizzle the creamy chipotle sauce generously over the top and serve with a fresh lime wedge.

title: MIXED GREENS WITH CHOICE DRESSING
steps: Wash and spin the mixed greens until completely dry, then hold chilled for service.
Prepare cucumbers, tomatoes, carrots, and red onion; keep vegetables chilled separately until assembly.
Combine greens and vegetables in a large bowl shortly before service.
Serve with house vinaigrette or the requested choice dressing on the side.

title: MAPLE PANNA COTTA

ingredients.name: Grade A dark or amber works best)Unflavored powdered gelatin ((approx. 2.25 tsp))

steps: 1. Bloom the GelatinPour the 2 tablespoons of cold water into a small bowl.Sprinkle the powdered gelatin evenly over the surface of the water.Let it sit undisturbed for 5 minutes. The gelatin will absorb the water and become thick and spongy.

2. Heat the Cream and Maple BaseIn a medium saucepan, combine the heavy whipping cream, whole milk, pure maple syrup, and a tiny pinch of salt.Place the saucepan over medium heat and stir occasionally.Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer. Do not let it boil. As soon as you see small bubbles forming around the edges, remove the pan from the heat.

3. Dissolve the Gelatin and VanillaAdd the bloomed gelatin and the vanilla extract directly into the hot cream mixture.Whisk continuously for 1 to 2 minutes until the gelatin is completely dissolved and no streaks remain.

4. Pour and StrainPour the mixture through a fine-mesh strainer into a large measuring cup with a pour spout. Straining ensures a perfectly velvety texture by catching any tiny undissolved gelatin bits.Divide the liquid evenly among 4 small dessert glasses, ramekins, or panna cotta molds.

5. Chill Until SetLet the glasses cool to room temperature on the counter for about 20 minutes.Cover each glass loosely with plastic wrap.Refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight, until the custard is firmly set with a delicate wobble.
6. Garnish and ServeServe the panna cotta cold directly in the glasses.Top each portion with an extra drizzle of pure maple syrup right before serving.

title: GINGER & CARROT WITH CORRIANDER YOGURT (GF)

ingredients.name: Carrots ((900g), peeled and chopped into 1/2-inch thick rounds)

steps: 1. Sauté the Aromatics and CarrotsHeat the olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat.Add the chopped onion and carrots. Sauté for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the onions are translucent and the carrots begin to soften slightly.Stir in the grated ginger, minced garlic, and 1 teaspoon of the ground coriander. Cook for 1 minute until the spices are highly fragrant.

2. Simmer the Soup BasePour the stock into the pot, scraping up any browned bits from the bottom.Add the sea salt and black pepper.Bring the liquid to a boil, then reduce the heat to low.Cover and simmer for 20 minutes, or until the carrot rounds are completely fork-tender.

3. Blend Until VelvetyRemove the pot from the heat.Use an immersion blender to puree the soup until it is completely smooth and creamy. Note: If using a stand blender, blend in small batches and leave the lid cap slightly open to let steam escape.Stir in the fresh lime juice, then taste and add more salt if needed.
4. Mix the Coriander YogurtIn a small bowl, whisk together the Greek yogurt, chopped fresh cilantro, and the remaining 1/2 teaspoon of ground coriander until smooth.
5. Garnish and ServeLadle the hot ginger-carrot soup into 4 individual serving bowls.Top each bowl with a beautiful swirl of the chilled coriander yogurt.

title: CHICKEN FRIED RICE

ingredients.name: Cooked white rice ((cold, leftover rice works best))
Chicken breast or thighs ((450g), boneless, skinless, cut into small bite-sized cubes)
Scallions (green onions) (thinly sliced (separate white and green parts))

steps: 1. Sear the ChickenHeat 1 tablespoon of neutral oil (or butter) in a large wok or skillet over high heat.Add the cubed chicken, salt, and black pepper.Stir-fry for 5 to 6 minutes until the chicken is golden-brown and cooked through. Transfer the chicken to a plate.

2. Scramble the EggsTurn the heat down to medium and add 1 teaspoon of oil to the same pan.Pour in the beaten eggs. Cook quickly, stirring constantly, until soft curds form (about 1 minute).Remove the eggs from the pan and place them on the plate with the chicken.

3. Sauté the Aromatics and VeggiesAdd the remaining oil or butter to the pan over high heat.Toss in the minced garlic and the white parts of the sliced scallions. Stir rapidly for 30 seconds until highly fragrant.Add the thawed peas and carrots, stir-frying for 1 to 2 minutes until hot.

4. Toss and Fry the RiceAdd the cold, cooked rice directly into the pan, breaking up any large clumps with your spatula.Pour the soy sauce and toasted sesame oil evenly over the rice.Stir-fry continuously for 3 to 4 minutes, pressing the rice against the hot pan so the grains toast and absorb the savory flavors.

5. Combine and FinishReturn the cooked chicken, scrambled eggs, and the green parts of the scallions back into the pan.Toss everything together for 1 final minute until completely incorporated and steaming hot.
6. Serve HotScoop the fried rice into bowls and serve immediately.

title: STEAMED COD WITH GINGER SCALLION SAUCE

ingredients.name: Fresh ginger (-inch piece (half sliced into matchsticks, half finely grated))
Scallions (green onions) ((4 cut into 2-inch julienne strips, 2 finely minced))
Neutral high-smoke point oil ((like peanut, canola, or avocado oil))
Cilantro (/4 cup, roughly chopped for garnish🥣)

steps: 1. Prep the Fish and SteamerPat the cod fillets completely dry with paper towels.Rub the Shaoxing rice wine lightly over both sides of the fillets.Prepare a heatproof plate that fits inside your steamer basket. Lay half of the ginger matchsticks and half of the julienned scallions on the plate, then set the cod fillets on top. Scatter the remaining ginger matchsticks over the fish.Bring 2 inches of water to a rolling boil in your steamer pot or wok over high heat.

2. Steam the CodPlace the plate with the fish inside the steamer basket.Cover tightly with a lid and steam over high heat for 7 to 9 minutes. The fish is done when it turns completely opaque and flakes easily with a fork.Carefully remove the plate from the steamer. Pour off and discard any liquid that pooled on the plate, as it can taste overly fishy.

3. Mix the Soy BaseIn a small bowl, whisk together the soy sauce, water, and sugar until the sugar completely dissolves.Pour this seasoned soy mixture directly around the base of the steamed fish on the plate.

4. Flash-Fry the AromaticsPile the finely grated ginger, minced scallions, and remaining julienned scallions directly on top of the cod fillets.Heat the 3 tablespoons of neutral oil in a small saucepan over high heat until it just begins to smoke lightly.Crucial Step: Carefully drizzle the hot oil directly over the raw ginger and scallions on the fish. It will sizzle loudly, instantly cooking the aromatics and releasing their fragrance into the fish.
5. Garnish and ServeScatter the fresh chopped cilantro over the top.Serve immediately, spooning the savory sauce from the bottom of the plate over each portion.

title: THAI CUCUMBER SALAD

ingredients.name: English cucumbers (thinly sliced into rounds or shaved into ribbons)
Red onion (/2 small, sliced into paper-thin slivers)
Granulated sugar ((substitute honey or palm sugar))
Fish sauce ((substitute soy sauce for a strict vegetarian option))
Thai bird's eye chili (seeded and finely minced (substitute 1/2 tsp red pepper flakes))

steps: 1. Mellow the Red OnionSoak the thinly sliced red onions in a small bowl of ice water for 5 minutes to remove their sharp bite.Drain and pat completely dry.
2. Whisk the Thai DressingIn a medium bowl, combine the rice vinegar, sugar, fish sauce, sesame oil, minced chili, and sea salt.Whisk vigorously until the sugar and salt are completely dissolved.

3. Toss the Salad ComponentsAdd the sliced cucumbers, dried red onions, and chopped cilantro into a large salad bowl.Pour the dressing over the vegetables and toss gently to ensure everything is evenly coated.

4. Garnish and ServeScatter the coarsely chopped roasted peanuts over the top right before serving to maintain their maximum crunch.Serve immediately, or let it chill in the refrigerator for 10 minutes to allow the flavors to deepen.

title: S'MORE CUPCAKES

ingredients.name: Hot water or brewed coffee (/2 cup)

steps: 1. Bake the Graham Cracker CrustPreheat your oven to 350°F (175°C) and line a 12-cup muffin tin with paper liners.In a small bowl, mix the finely crushed graham cracker crumbs, melted butter, and 1 tablespoon of granulated sugar until it feels like wet sand.Spoon an even tablespoon of the mixture into the bottom of each liner. Press down firmly with the bottom of a small glass to pack it tight.Bake for 5 minutes until fragrant and lightly golden. Remove from the oven and let cool while making the batter.

2. Whisk the Chocolate BatterIn a large bowl, whisk together the flour, cocoa powder, remaining sugar (minus 3/4 cup reserved for frosting), baking powder, baking soda, and salt.In a separate bowl, whisk the egg, whole milk, vegetable oil, and 1 teaspoon of vanilla extract.Pour the wet ingredients into the dry ingredients and stir gently until just combined.Slowly stir in the hot water or coffee until the batter is smooth. Note: The batter will be very thin, but this ensures the cupcakes turn out incredibly moist.

3. Bake the CupcakesDivide the chocolate batter evenly among the 12 liners, filling each about 2/3 of the way full over the graham crust.Bake for 16 to 18 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the center of a cupcake comes out clean.Transfer the cupcakes to a wire rack to cool completely before frosting.

4. Whip the Marshmallow MeringueCreate a double boiler by placing a heatproof glass bowl over a saucepan filled with 1 inch of simmering water. Ensure the bottom of the bowl does not touch the water.Add the 3 egg whites, the remaining 3/4 cup of granulated sugar, and the cream of tartar to the bowl. Whisk constantly for 3 to 4 minutes until the sugar is completely dissolved and the mixture is warm to the touch.Remove the bowl from the heat. Add the remaining 1/2 teaspoon of vanilla extract.Use an electric hand mixer on high speed to beat the mixture for 5 to 7 minutes until it forms thick, glossy, stiff peaks that look like marshmallow fluff.

5. Frost and ToastSpoon or pipe a generous mound of the marshmallow frosting onto each cooled cupcake.Crucial Step: Use a kitchen culinary torch to gently toast the outside of the marshmallow frosting until it is beautifully browned and caramelized. If you do not have a torch, place the frosted cupcakes under your oven's broiler on high for 1 to 2 minutes, watching them very closely so they do not catch fire.
6. Garnish and ServeTop each cupcake with a small piece of a Hershey's chocolate bar and a tiny sprinkle of leftover graham cracker crumbs before serving.

title: HERB ROASTED CHICKEN THIGH OVER ROASTED SPUDS (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: Russet or Yukon Gold potatoes ((900g), peeled and cut into thick wedges)
Paprika (/2 tsp (adds beautiful color to the chicken skin))

steps: 1. Prep and Season the ComponentsPreheat your oven to 425°F (220°C).In a small bowl, whisk together the olive oil, fresh lemon juice, minced garlic, dried oregano, dried thyme, garlic powder, paprika, sea salt, and black pepper to create a vibrant marinade.Place the potato wedges into a large bowl. Pour half of the olive oil marinade over them and toss thoroughly until every wedge is evenly coated.

2. Arrange the Roasting PanTransfer the seasoned potato wedges into a large 9x13-inch baking dish or a rimmed baking sheet, spreading them out into a flat, uniform layer.Pat the chicken thighs completely dry with paper towels. Note: Removing surface moisture is the secret to achieving perfectly crispy chicken skin.Rub the remaining half of the marinade all over the chicken thighs, making sure to get underneath the skin.Nestled the chicken thighs directly on top of the bed of potatoes, skin-side up.

3. Roast to PerfectionPlace the pan into the preheated oven and roast uncovered for 40 to 45 minutes.As the chicken cooks, its savory juices will drip down and flavor the potatoes, rendering them perfectly tender on the inside with crispy edges.The dish is done when the chicken skin is deeply golden-brown and a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the thigh reads 175°F (80°C). Chicken thighs handle higher internal temperatures well, making them incredibly juicy and tender.

4. Rest and ServeRemove the pan from the oven.Let the chicken rest in the pan for 5 minutes before serving to let the juices redistribute.Plate a generous scoop of the roasted spuds, place a crispy chicken thigh over the top, and spoon any leftover lemon-garlic pan juices from the bottom of the dish over the meat.

title: RATATOUILLE WITH BRAISED LENTILS (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: Brown or French green lentils (rinsed (do not use red lentils, as they turn mushy))
Chinese or Japanese eggplant (sliced into 1/8-inch thin rounds)

steps: 1. Braise the LentilsHeat 1 tablespoon of olive oil in a medium saucepan over medium heat.Add the diced onion and half of the minced garlic (2 cloves). Sauté for 3 to 4 minutes until translucent.Stir in the rinsed lentils, 1/2 teaspoon of Italian seasoning, and a pinch of salt.Pour in the vegetable stock and add the fresh thyme sprigs. Bring to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 25 minutes until the lentils are tender but still hold their shape. Drain any excess liquid and discard the thyme stems.

2. Prepare the Sauce BasePreheat your oven to 375°F (190°C).In a small bowl, mix the crushed tomatoes with the remaining minced garlic, 1/2 teaspoon of Italian seasoning, and a pinch of salt and pepper.Spread this seasoned tomato sauce evenly across the bottom of an 8x8-inch square or 9-inch round baking dish.

3. Arrange the VegetablesAlternating the slices, stack a piece of eggplant, zucchini, yellow squash, and Roma tomato.Arrange the veggie stacks tightly in rows (or a spiral pattern) on top of the tomato sauce until the pan is fully packed.

4. Season and BakeDrizzle the remaining 2 tablespoons of olive oil evenly over the top of the arranged vegetables.Sprinkle with the remaining 1/2 teaspoon of Italian seasoning, sea salt, and black pepper.Cover the baking dish tightly with aluminum foil to trap the steam.Bake for 30 minutes, then remove the foil and bake uncovered for an additional 10 to 15 minutes until the vegetables are completely tender and slightly browned at the edges.

5. Assemble and ServeSpoon a generous bed of the hot, savory braised lentils into 4 individual serving bowls.Use a wide spatula to carefully lift a portion of the roasted ratatouille vegetables and place them directly over the lentils. Spoon any extra warm tomato sauce from the baking dish over the top.

title: PROSCIUTTO & MELON WITH BALSAMIC REDUCTION

ingredients.name: Cantaloupe melon (ripe melon (peeled, seeded, and cut into 12 thick wedges))
Balsamic vinegar (/2 cup (standard quality works great for reducing))
Fresh mint leaves (/4 cup, torn or chiffonade for garnish)
Arugula (washed and dried (optional, for plating base))

steps: 1. Simmer the Balsamic ReductionPour the 1/2 cup of balsamic vinegar into a small saucepan over medium-low heat.Bring the vinegar to a gentle simmer. Do not let it boil vigorously.Simmer uncovered for 8 to 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the liquid reduces by half. It should coat the back of a spoon and have a syrupy, glossy consistency.Pour the reduction into a small bowl and let it cool completely. It will continue to thicken as it cools.
2. Prep the MelonCut the cantaloupe in half, scoop out the seeds with a spoon, and slice it into wedges.Cut off the outer rind from each wedge, leaving clean, crescent-shaped melon pieces.

3. Wrap the ProsciuttoTake one slice of prosciutto and wrap it loosely around the center of a melon wedge.Repeat this step until all 12 melon wedges are neatly wrapped. Chef's Tip: Let the prosciutto sit at room temperature for 10 minutes before wrapping so the slices separate easily without tearing.

4. Assemble and GarnishSpread a light bed of optional arugula across a large serving platter.Arrange the wrapped prosciutto and melon wedges neatly over the top.Drizzle the cooled, thickened balsamic reduction in zig-zag lines across the entire platter.Scatter the fresh torn mint leaves over the top and finish with a generous crack of fresh black pepper. Serve immediately.

title: VANILLA CRÈME BRULEE

ingredients.name: Granulated sugar (/2 cup (divided: 1/4 cup for the custard, 1/4 cup for the torched sugar crust))
Vanilla bean ((or substitute 2 tsp pure vanilla bean paste))
A tiny pinchBoiling water (Approx. 4 to 5 cups (for the water bath)🥣)

steps: 1. Infuse the CreamPreheat your oven to 325°F (165°C). Place 4 standard 6-ounce ramekins inside a large, deep baking pan (like a 9x13-inch metal pan).Pour the heavy cream and a tiny pinch of salt into a medium saucepan.Use a sharp knife to split the vanilla bean lengthwise. Scrape out the tiny black seeds with the back of the knife and add both the seeds and the empty pod into the cream.Place the pan over medium heat until it just begins to simmer around the edges. Remove from heat, cover with a lid, and let it steep for 10 minutes. Remove and discard the vanilla bean pod.
2. Whisk the Yolks and SugarIn a medium bowl, whisk together the 5 egg yolks and 1/4 cup of the granulated sugar until the mixture becomes pale yellow and slightly thickened (about 1 to 2 minutes).

3. Temper the EggsWhile whisking the egg mixture continuously, slowly drizzle in a small splash of the warm vanilla cream. This warms the eggs gradually so they do not scramble.Continue to slowly pour in the remaining cream in a steady stream, whisking constantly until completely combined.Pour the final custard liquid through a fine-mesh strainer into a large measuring cup with a pour spout to catch any bubbles or tiny egg bits.

4. Bake in a Water Bath (Bain-Marie)Divide the strained custard evenly among the 4 ramekins in the baking pan.Carefully pour boiling water into the large baking pan around the ramekins, being careful not to splash any water into the custard. Fill the pan until the water reaches halfway up the sides of the ramekins. This gentle steam-cooking prevents the custard from cracking or curdling.Bake for 30 to 35 minutes. The custards are done when the edges are set, but the absolute center still has a distinct, gelatin-like wobble.

5. Chill ThoroughlyCarefully remove the ramekins from the hot water bath and let them cool to room temperature on a wire rack for 30 minutes.Cover each ramekin with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or ideally overnight, until completely cold and firm.

6. Torch the Sugar CrustRemove the custards from the refrigerator right before serving and blot any surface condensation gently with a paper towel.Sprinkle exactly 1 tablespoon of the remaining granulated sugar evenly over the surface of each cold custard, tilting the ramekin to ensure full coverage.Use a culinary kitchen torch to melt the sugar using a slow, circular motion. The sugar will bubble, liquefy, and turn into a deep amber-brown caramel crust.Let the caramel sit undisturbed for 2 minutes to cool and harden into a glass-like sheet before serving.

title: CHICKEN VEGETABLE BROTH (GF)
steps: Bring chicken stock to a simmer in a soup pot and add diced chicken.
Simmer gently until chicken is cooked through, skimming the surface as needed.
Add carrots, celery, onion, and zucchini; cook until vegetables are tender but not mushy.
Season with salt and pepper, finish with parsley, and hold hot for service.

title: THAI GRILLED CHICKEN BREAST (GF/DF)

ingredients.name: Chicken breasts ((700g), boneless, skinless, pounded to an even 1/2-inch thickness)
Fresh cilantro roots or stems (/4 cup, finely chopped (stems hold the most flavor))
Ground white pepper ((substitute black pepper if needed))
Ground turmeric (/2 tsp (adds a beautiful golden hue))

steps: 1. Paste the AromaticsIn a mortar and pestle (or a small food processor), combine the minced lemongrass, garlic, chopped cilantro stems, white pepper, ground coriander, and turmeric.Grind vigorously until the ingredients break down into a thick, highly aromatic paste.

2. Marinate the ChickenIn a large bowl or zip-top bag, whisk together the fresh aromatic paste, fish sauce, tamari, palm sugar, and toasted sesame oil until the sugar dissolves.Add the pounded chicken breasts, tossing thoroughly to ensure every piece is completely coated.Seal and refrigerate for at least 1 to 2 hours (ideally 4 hours) to let the flavors deeply penetrate the meat.

3. Grill to PerfectionPreheat your outdoor grill or an indoor cast-iron grill pan to medium-high heat. Lightly oil the grates.Remove the chicken from the marinade, shaking off any excess chunks of lemongrass that might burn easily.Place the chicken breasts on the grill.
