steps: Dredge and egg coat: Mix the gluten-free flour, kosher salt, and white pepper together in a shallow dish. Whisk the eggs and water together in a second shallow bowl.

Pat the chicken breasts completely dry with paper towels. Coat each piece lightly in the flour mixture, shaking off excess, and then submerge completely in the beaten egg mixture so it is fully coated in egg wash.Pan-fry golden: Heat the olive oil and 1 tablespoon of the dairy-free butter alternative together in a large skillet over medium-high heat until hot and foaming.

Lift the chicken from the egg wash, letting the excess drip off, and place directly into the skillet. Fry for 3 to 4 minutes per side until the egg coating is puffed, golden brown, and the chicken is cooked through.

Transfer the chicken to a warm plate and cover with foil.Simmer wine reduction: Pour the white wine, chicken broth, and fresh lemon juice directly into the hot skillet over medium heat. Use a wooden spoon to scrape up any savory browned bits stuck to the bottom of the pan.

Bring the liquid to a rapid boil and let it cook uncovered for 5 minutes until reduced by half. Drop the fresh lemon slices into the bubbling sauce for the final 2 minutes of simmering.Thicken glaze and serve: Turn the heat down to low.

Whisk in the remaining 1 tablespoon of dairy-free butter alternative and the chopped fresh parsley until the sauce becomes smooth, glossy, and thickens slightly. Return the cooked chicken breasts and their accumulated plate juices back into the skillet, spooning the velvety lemon sauce over the meat for 1 minute until glazed and hot.
Serve immediately.

title: Vegetable Enchiladas with Melted Cheese

ingredients.name: Monterey Jack or sharp Cheddar cheese (shredded, divided)

steps: Preheat and cook filling: Preheat your oven to 375°F (190°C) and lightly grease a 9x13-inch baking dish. Heat the olive oil in a large skillet over medium heat.

Sauté the diced zucchini, yellow bell pepper, and red onion for 5 to 6 minutes until they begin to soften.Add aromatics and beans: Stir in the minced garlic, chili powder, cumin, salt, and black pepper, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant. Remove the skillet from the heat source and fold in the black beans, corn kernels, and ½ cup of the shredded cheese until evenly distributed.Assemble the enchiladas: Pour ½ cup of the red enchilada sauce across the bottom of the prepared baking dish to create a non-stick layer.

Dip each warm tortilla lightly into the remaining sauce to coat, spoon a generous portion of the veggie filling down the center, and roll up tightly. Place the rolled enchiladas seam-side down in the baking dish.Bake and melt cheese: Pour the remaining enchilada sauce smoothly over the top of all the rolled tortillas, ensuring the edges are well coated so they don't dry out.

Sprinkle the remaining 1½ cups of shredded cheese evenly over the sauce. Bake uncovered for 20 to 25 minutes until the sauce is bubbling at the edges and the cheese layer is completely melted, gooey, and lightly golden-brown.
Garnish with chopped cilantro and sliced green onions before serving hot.

title: PEACH FRIED PIES (DF)

ingredients.name: ice water, plus 1 to 2 tablespoons if needed

steps: Combine the chopped peaches, granulated sugar, brown sugar, cornstarch, lemon juice, cinnamon, nutmeg, and 0.25 teaspoon salt in a saucepan. Simmer over medium heat for 6 to 8 minutes, stirring constantly, until glossy and jam-like. Transfer to a bowl and cool completely.
Whisk the flour with the remaining 1 teaspoon salt. Cut in the chilled vegetable shortening until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs with pea-sized pieces.

Drizzle in 0.5 cup ice water and mix gently with a fork just until the dough comes together, adding 1 tablespoon extra water at a time only if needed. Shape into a disc, wrap, and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Roll the chilled dough on a lightly floured surface to about 1/8-inch thickness. Cut eight 5-inch rounds, rerolling scraps as needed.
Place 2 tablespoons cooled filling in the center of each round. Lightly moisten the edge, fold into a half-moon, and crimp firmly with a floured fork.
Heat the vegetable oil in a deep skillet or Dutch oven to 350 F. Fry 2 or 3 pies at a time for 3 to 4 minutes per side, turning once, until puffed, crisp, and deeply golden.
Drain on paper towels and cool for 5 to 10 minutes. Dust with powdered sugar if desired and serve warm or at room temperature.

title: BLUEBERRY CRUMBLE CHEESECAKE

ingredients.name: cream cheese, softened to room temperature

steps: Heat the oven to 325 F and lightly grease a 9-inch springform pan. Mix the graham crumbs, 3 tablespoons of the granulated sugar, and melted butter. Press firmly into the bottom and slightly up the sides; bake for 10 minutes and cool completely.
Combine the blueberries, 2 tablespoons granulated sugar, cornstarch, and lemon juice in a saucepan. Cook over medium heat for 5 to 6 minutes, gently mashing the berries, until glossy and thick. Cool.
Whisk together the flour, brown sugar, and cinnamon. Cut in the cold cubed butter until coarse clumps form, then refrigerate the crumble.

Beat the softened cream cheese with 1 cup granulated sugar for 2 to 3 minutes until smooth. Mix in the sour cream and vanilla. On low speed, add the eggs one at a time, mixing only until each yolk disappears.
Pour the cheesecake batter over the cooled crust. Spoon the blueberry mixture over the batter and swirl gently with a knife. Sprinkle the chilled crumble evenly over the surface.

Wrap the bottom of the pan in foil and bake for 50 minutes, until the edges are set and the center still jiggles slightly. Turn off the oven, crack the door 2 inches, and cool the cheesecake inside for 1 hour.
Remove the foil, run a thin knife around the inside edge, and refrigerate for at least 6 hours or overnight before removing the springform collar and slicing.

title: OSSO BUCCO WITH SPICED AVOCADO LIME SAUCE
steps: Preheat and sear: Preheat oven to 325°F (160°C). Season veal shanks heavily with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a Dutch oven; sear shanks for 4 minutes per side until deeply browned; remove.
Saute and deglaze: Cook diced onion in the same pot for 3 minutes. Pour in white wine, scraping the bottom, and simmer 3 minutes. Add beef broth.
Braised tender: Return veal shanks to the liquid, cover tightly with a lid, and braise in the oven for 2 hours until fork-tender.
Blend sauce and serve: Blend avocados, lime juice, jalapeno, cilantro, garlic, cumin, and salt until ultra-smooth. Slice veal and serve hot with a pool of the cold, creamy avocado sauce.

title: GRILLED SALMON WITH BASIL PESTO/ROASTED TOMATO
steps: Preheat and roast: Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Toss cherry tomatoes with 1 Tbsp olive oil, balsamic vinegar, salt, and pepper on a baking sheet; roast for 15 minutes until blistered; remove.
Make the pesto: Blend basil leaves, toasted pine nuts, garlic, parmesan cheese, and extra virgin olive oil in a food processor until ultra-smooth. Season with salt to taste.

Grill the salmon: Preheat grill to medium-high heat. Brush salmon fillets with remaining olive oil and season heavily with salt and pepper; grill skin-side down for 5 minutes, flip, and grill 4 minutes more until cooked through.
Assemble and serve: Place grilled salmon fillets on a platter. Spoon a generous layer of basil pesto over the top and scatter the warm roasted tomatoes over the sauce.

title: NO BAKE ECLAIR CAKE

ingredients.name: instant vanilla pudding mix (3.4 oz each)

steps: Whisk and fold: Whisk together instant vanilla pudding mix and 3 cups of whole milk in a large bowl for 2 minutes until thickened. Gently fold in the thawed whipped topping until the filling is uniform and smooth.
Layer the base: Arrange a single layer of graham crackers in the bottom of a 9x13-inch baking dish, breaking them to fit snugly. Spread half of the pudding mixture evenly over the cracker layer.
Repeat and top: Add a second layer of graham crackers over the pudding, followed by the remaining pudding mixture. Place a final layer of graham crackers on top and press down very lightly.

Boil and chill: Melt butter, cocoa powder, sugar, and 0.25 cup milk in a saucepan over medium heat, boiling for 1 minute while stirring. Remove from heat, stir in vanilla, and pour the warm glaze over the top; chill cake for 4 hours.

title: TOMATO CHEDDAR AND BACON PIE
steps: Preheat and crisp: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Fry diced bacon in a skillet over medium heat for 7 minutes until completely crisp; remove and drain on paper towels.
Drain and dry: Layer sliced tomatoes on paper towels and sprinkle heavily with salt; let sit for 15 minutes to draw out excess moisture, then blot dry.
Mix the topping: Combine shredded cheddar, mozzarella, mayonnaise, chopped basil, minced garlic, and pepper in a medium bowl until thoroughly mixed.
Layer and bake: Layer tomatoes and crisped bacon inside the pre-baked pie crust. Spread the cheese mixture evenly over the top layer; bake for 30 minutes until bubbling and golden brown.

title: BEEF STROGANOFF (GF)

ingredients.name: beef sirloin steak (sliced into thin strips)

steps: Preheat and sear: Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Season beef strips heavily with salt and pepper; sear shanks and strips for 3 minutes until browned but tender; remove.
Saute and deglaze: Cook diced onion and sliced mushrooms in the same pot for 5 minutes until soft. Pour in gluten-free beef broth and Worcestershire sauce, scraping the bottom, and simmer 3 minutes.
Thicken and simmer: Whisk cornstarch and cold water in a small bowl until smooth. Pour mixture into the simmering broth, stirring constantly, and let boil for 1 minute until the gravy thickens up.
Finish and serve: Lower heat to low. Stir in the sour cream and returned beef strips until ultra-smooth and hot (do not boil). Slice veal and serve hot over cooked gluten-free noodles.

title: FARO WITH FRESH CHOPPED HERBS
steps: Rinse and boil: Rinse farro thoroughly under cold water. Combine farro, water, and a pinch of salt in a medium saucepan over high heat; bring to a rolling boil.
Simmer and tender: Lower heat to medium-low, cover tightly with a lid, and simmer for 25 minutes until the farro grains are chewy and tender.
Drain and cool: Drain any excess water completely from the pot. Transfer the cooked farro to a large mixing bowl, toss with olive oil, and let cool for 10 minutes.
Toss and serve: Add the chopped parsley, mint, dill, green onions, and lemon juice into the bowl; season with salt and pepper, and toss until ultra-smooth and well combined.

title: LOADED BAKED POTATO
steps: Preheat and bake: Preheat oven to 400°F (205°C). Pierce clean potatoes all over with a fork, rub skins heavily with olive oil and salt, and bake directly on the oven rack for 1 hour until tender.
Crisp the bacon: Fry diced bacon in a skillet over medium heat for 7 minutes until completely crisp; remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
Slice and fluff: Slice a thin layer off the top of each baked potato lengthwise. Gently fluff the steaming inside flesh with a fork, mashing it slightly while keeping the outer skin intact.
Load and serve: Stuff each potato with 1 Tbsp butter, seasoned with pepper. Top evenly with shredded cheddar cheese, warm crisped bacon, a dollop of cold sour cream, and fresh chopped chives.
